climate: Best time: April-May and October-November, temperatures of 18-26°C ideal for city visits. Avoid July-August: scorching heat (often 38-42°C in Seville and Córdoba). Mild but rainy winter, Granada can be chilly.

title: Secret Granada: Alpujarras, Guadix and the Sierras
subtitle: A 5 days slow travel journey through the white Berber villages of the Alpujarras, the cave dwellings of Guadix, and the fifth holy city of Christianity at Caravaca de la Cruz.
keywords: caravaca de la cruz holy city
climate: Best period: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-28°C, ideal for hiking the Alpujarras. Avoid July-August: scorching heat in Guadix and Baza plains (35-40°C), though mountain villages stay cooler. Winter is cold at altitude with possible snow above 1500m.

title: Cádiz Road Trip: Jerez, Sherry Bodegas & White Villages
subtitle: From the oldest city in the Western world to legendary sherry cellars, mountain-perched white villages, and the wild marshlands of Doñana
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 20-28°C, ideal light for the coast and white villages. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 35-40°C, Doñana unbearable). Mild winter (15°C) but windy, occasional rain.

title: Aragonese Pyrenees and Lower Aragón: From Ordesa to Teruel
subtitle: A 6 days road trip through the heart of Aragón, from Jaca's citadel to Ordesa's canyons, from the perched medieval villages of Upper Aragón to Daroca's ramparts, from the 'Aragonese Tuscany' of Matarraña to the Templar commanderies of Maestrazgo, all the way to Teruel's Mudéjar towers.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C in lowlands, pleasant coolness in the mountains. Avoid December-March: Pyrenean passes closed by snow, Teruel experiences harsh winters (-5 to 5°C). July-August brings intense heat in Lower Aragon (35°C+), though bearable at altitude.

title: Aragon Circuit: Zaragoza & Mudéjar Villages
subtitle: From Goya's domes to glazed-brick towers, from rose sandstone villages to Romanesque fortresses perched atop the sierras
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-28°C. Avoid July-August (scorching heat, 35-40°C in Zaragoza, dry cierzo wind). Cold winters at altitude (Teruel, Albarracín), possible snow December-February.

title: Asturias Road Trip: Cantabrian Coast and Picos de Europa
subtitle: From the pre-Romanesque vaults of Mount Naranco to the glacial lakes of Covadonga, from fishing villages to the painted cliffs of Llanes
climate: Best time: June-September, temperatures 18-24°C on the coast, cooler at altitude (10-18°C in the Picos). Avoid October-April: frequent rain, fog in the mountains, mountain roads sometimes closed. Green Spain stays humid even in summer.

title: Lanzarote & La Palma: Where Fire Shaped Art and Nature
subtitle: From Timanfaya's sea of lava to the laurel forests of Caldera de Taburiente—two volcanic islands with opposite souls.
climate: Best time: March-June and September-November, 20-25°C with excellent sunshine. Lanzarote stays dry year-round, La Palma wetter at altitude. Avoid July-August (strong trade winds, heat) and December-February (frequent rain in La Palma, 17°C).

title: Canary Islands Road Trip: Tenerife & Gran Canaria
subtitle: From the volcanic summit of Mount Teide to Atlantic pools carved into lava, from the colonial streets of Vegueta to the Saharan dunes of Maspalomas
climate: Mild climate year-round, best April-June and September-November (22-26°C, light winds). Avoid July-August (intense heat up to 30-35°C, tourist crowds, possible calima dust). North cloudier in winter, south dry and sunny.

title: Cantabria Road Trip: Atlantic Bays and Picos de Europa
subtitle: From Santander to Castro Urdiales, through medieval villages, Catalan modernism and the peaks of the Picos de Europa
climate: Best time: May to September, temperatures 18-25°C on the coast, cooler at altitude. Avoid November-March: frequent rain, mountain roads to Fuente Dé challenging. Picos de Europa snow-covered until May.

title: Castellón and Maestrazgo Route: Castles and Coves
subtitle: From the Templar castle of Peñíscola to the wild coves of the Sierra d'Irta, between perched medieval villages and golden beaches of the Costa del Azahar.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 20-28°C on the coast, cooler in Morella (15-22°C). Avoid July-August: scorching heat on the coast (30-35°C) and mass tourism. Mild coastal winters but cold in the mountains (frequent frost in Morella).

title: Madrid to Cazorla: La Mancha, the Sierras of Jaén, and the Forgotten Renaissance
subtitle: A 8 days road trip through Spain's great tourist void, from Madrid's museums to the country's largest natural park, via Almagro's Golden Age theatre, the UNESCO Renaissance towns of Úbeda and Baeza, and the thousand-year-old Caliphate castle of Baños de la Encina.
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, temperatures 18-25°C throughout. Avoid July-August at all costs: scorching heat in La Mancha and Jaén (35-42°C), only the Cazorla mountains offer relief. Cold, dry winters on the plateau, snow possible in the mountains.

title: Alcarria and Guadalajara: Between Madrid and Aragon
subtitle: A 5 days itinerary through the great tourist void between Madrid and Zaragoza, from Cervantes' university town to the ramparts of Daroca, via the lavender fields of Alcarria, the fortress-cathedral of Sigüenza, and Medinaceli's unique Roman triple arch.
keywords: Santa María de Huerta Cistercian monastery
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, with pleasant temperatures of 15-25°C for exploring historic towns. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 35-40°C, drought) and January-February (frost, cold cierzo wind in Aragon). Semi-arid region with limited rainfall.

title: La Mancha Road Trip: Don Quixote's Windmills and Cuenca
subtitle: From Cuenca to Ruidera, between vertiginous gorges, Cervantine windmills and turquoise lagoons in the heart of the Meseta
cities: parque natural de las lagunas de ruidera
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-26°C, clear skies perfect for windmill viewing. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 35-40°C, bone-dry). Winter is cold in Cuenca gorges (0-10°C), possible snow.

title: The Don Quixote Route: A Road Trip Through Castilla-La Mancha's Windmills & Plains
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, low rainfall. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 35-40°C on the La Mancha plateau). Cold winters with possible frost in Toledo and Cuenca.

title: Castilian Duero: From Valladolid to Cuenca via Ribera and Alcarria
subtitle: A 7 days road trip through Spain's forgotten heartland, exploring baroque polychrome sculpture, Reconquest castles, Ribera del Duero vineyards, ducal villages, and Alcarria's lavender fields.
keywords: medina del campo la mota castle
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-28°C, clear skies. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 35-40°C, parched landscape) and December-February (dry cold, frequent frost). Low rainfall year-round across the Meseta plateau.

title: Castile and León Road Trip: Cathedrals and Medieval Villages
subtitle: From Covarrubias to Astorga, five stops exploring half-timbered medieval towns, soaring Gothic cathedrals, and Gaudí's whimsical palace along the Camino de Santiago
climate: Best period: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C. Avoid July-August (dry heat up to 35°C) and December-February (frequent frost, icy winds on the plateau). Large day-night temperature swings in spring.

title: Barcelona and Costa Brava Road Trip: Coves and Villages
subtitle: From Gaudí to the cliffs of Cap de Creus, 8 days exploring Catalan modernism, medieval villages, and turquoise coves.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 20-27°C, pleasant sea and moderate crowds. Avoid July-August (intense heat in Barcelona up to 35°C, packed beaches, high prices). Winter stays mild (12-15°C) but rainy, with frequent Tramontane winds along the coast.

title: Córdoba and the Sierra Morena: From the Caliphate to the Dehesas of Los Pedroches
subtitle: A 5 days itinerary from Córdoba, between the Mezquita and Medina Azahara, the castle of Almodóvar, the forests of the Sierra Morena, the tallest tower in the peninsula at Belalcázar, and the granite villages of Los Pedroches where Iberian pigs graze beneath holm oaks.
climate: Best time: April-May and October-November, temperatures 18-26°C. Avoid July-August (scorching heat in Córdoba, often 40-45°C). Mild winter (10-15°C) but rainy in Sierra Morena.

title: The Most Beautiful Villages of León Province
subtitle: A loop from León through El Bierzo, the Ancares highlands, La Maragatería, and the Cantabrian mountains.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, plenty of sunshine. Avoid July-August (scorching heat in León, up to 35°C) and December-February (frequent frost in the mountains). Continental climate with moderate spring rainfall.

title: Castile and León: Ramparts, Canyons and Peaks
subtitle: From Salamanca to the Sierra de Gredos, a grand tour through medieval treasures, wild gorges and high-mountain landscapes across Spain's largest autonomous community.
cities: parque natural de arribes del duero
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 18-26°C on the plains, cooler in Sierra de Gredos. Avoid July-August (scorching heat, 35°C+) and December-February (harsh cold, frequent snow in mountains). The Castilian plateau is dry with strong thermal amplitude.

title: Sierra de Francia Medieval Villages Route
subtitle: From Miranda del Castañar's intact ramparts to the Arribes del Duero cliffs, 6 days exploring Salamanca's best-preserved medieval villages.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for hiking the sierra. Avoid July-August (dry heat up to 35°C in valleys, scarce shade). Cold winters (0-8°C) with frequent mountain fog.

title: Secret Extremadura: From Plasencia to Zafra Through Valleys and Dehesas
subtitle: An 9 days slow travel road trip through the two hidden halves of Extremadura, weaving between cherry orchards in bloom, medieval Jewish quarters, an imperial monastery, Roman ramparts, and Portuguese Manueline architecture.
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, temperatures 20-28°C, abundant sunshine. Avoid July-August: extreme heat (35-40°C), exposed roads with no shade across the dehesas. Winter is mild but mornings can be chilly in northern valleys.

title: Extremadura Road Trip: Cáceres, Mérida & Trujillo
subtitle: UNESCO medieval cities, Roman ruins, and soaring birds of prey in Spain's hidden heartland
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for cultural visits and vulture-watching in Monfragüe. Avoid July-August: scorching heat (35-40°C) makes sightseeing exhausting. Winter is mild (10-15°C) but can be rainy.

title: Fuerteventura Road Trip: Dunes, Lagoons & Timeless Villages
subtitle: From the port capital to the endless dunes of Corralejo, the turquoise lagoons of El Cotillo, the ancient mountain capital of Betancuria, and the vast beaches of the Jandía Peninsula.
climate: Best period: April to October (22-28°C) for beaches and lagoons. Avoid January-March for swimming (cool water, strong winds). Constant trade winds: ideal for kitesurfing, but bring a windbreaker for evenings.

title: Galicia Road Trip: Santiago de Compostela and the Coast of Death
subtitle: From the pilgrim's cathedral to wild Atlantic sanctuaries—a journey between sacred ground and the edge of the Iberian world
climate: Best period: June to September, temperatures 18-25°C, ideal for Costa da Morte. Avoid October to April (heavy rainfall, frequent fog, rough seas). May is still rainy but acceptable if you can handle humidity.

title: Inner Galicia: From Ourense to the Ribeira Sacra
subtitle: A 5 days journey through the heart of Galicia, from the thermal springs of Ourense to the vertiginous canyons of the Sil, weaving between Romanesque monasteries, terraced vineyards, and ancient pallozas on the Camino de Santiago.
climate: Best period: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for exploring the Ribeira Sacra gorges. Avoid July-August (scorching heat in Ourense, up to 38°C) and December-February (persistent rain, possible snow at high-altitude O Cebreiro). The Sil and Miño valleys enjoy a drier microclimate than Galicia's coast.

title: Majorca Road Trip: Serra de Tramuntana & Hidden Coves
subtitle: From Palma's Gothic cathedral to Ibiza's turquoise bays, through mountain villages of the Tramuntana and Alcúdia's golden beaches.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 20-28°C, warm sea, fewer crowds. Avoid July-August: intense heat (30-35°C), overcrowded sites, high prices. Mild winter (12-16°C) but windy, possible rain in the Serra de Tramuntana.

title: Jaén's Renaissance Jewels: Olive Grove Landscapes & Wild Sierras of Andalusia
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, temperatures 20-28°C, ideal for Renaissance towns and Sierra de Cazorla hikes. Avoid July-August: scorching heat (35-40°C) in olive plains. Mild winter in valleys (10-15°C), possible snow in Sierra.

title: Madrid & Castile Road Trip: Royal Palaces, Ancient Walls & World-Class Museums
subtitle: Art capital, UNESCO medieval towns, and royal gardens in the heart of Spain
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, clear skies. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 35-40°C in historic towns with little shade). Winter is cold with possible frost in Segovia and Avila (1000m altitude).

title: Sierra de Guadarrama Road Trip: Castles and Forests
subtitle: From the medieval castles of Manzanares to the mountain trails of Navacerrada, from the Roman forests of Cercedilla to the monastery of Rascafría, and on to the ramparts of Buitrago overlooking the Lozoya River.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C in valleys, 10°C cooler at altitude. Avoid July-August (intense heat at lower elevations, frequent mountain storms) and December-March (snow, closed passes, -5 to 5°C). Spring perfect for castles and hiking.

title: Menorca Road Trip: Harbors, Whitewashed Villages & Hidden Coves
subtitle: From the gleaming white lanes of Binibeca to the wild cliffs of Cala Galdana, a journey through natural harbors and pristine turquoise coves
climate: Best period: May-June and September-October, temperatures 20-26°C, pleasant sea and accessible coves. Avoid July-August (intense heat 30-35°C, massive crowds on beaches). Mild but windy winter, frequent tramontana on the north coast.

title: Murcia Road Trip: Baroque Splendor & Costa Cálida
subtitle: Where flamboyant baroque façades, ancient Roman theaters, and turquoise coves of the Costa Cálida converge in brilliant sunlight.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, 20-28°C, maximum sunshine. Avoid July-August: scorching heat (35-40°C inland), oppressive even on the coast. Europe's driest region, almost no rainfall.

title: Bay of Biscay Coastal Drive: Fishing Villages & Basque Culinary Soul in France
climate: Best time: May-June and September, temperatures 18-23°C, fewer crowds. Avoid November-March (persistent rain, 150-200mm/month). Summer is decent (20-25°C) but crowded; oceanic climate brings rain even in July-August.

title: La Rioja Circuit: Wine Route and Monasteries
subtitle: From Logroño, the pintxo capital, to Sajazarra, a medieval gem—through century-old bodegas and UNESCO monasteries
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for wine tasting. Avoid July-August (heat often exceeding 35°C, dry). Cold winter with frequent frosts (0-10°C).

title: Castile-León and Galicia: 21 days from Roman aqueducts to the Atlantic
subtitle: Three weeks across high Castile to reach the end of the world, follow Galicia's Atlantic coast, and return via the Camino Francés—18 cities, 5 UNESCO sites, two seas, and three magnificent cathedrals.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, 18 to 26°C, less rain in Galicia. Avoid July-August (scorching heat in Castile, up to 38°C) and November-March (cold in León/Burgos, constant rain on the Atlantic coast).

title: Southern Catalonia in 10 Days: Tarragona, Priorat & the Ebro Delta
subtitle: Ten days crossing southern Catalonia, from Tarraco's Roman ruins to Priorat's steep vineyards, via Reus modernisme and the Ebro Delta rice paddies.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 20-28°C. Avoid July-August (intense heat 32-38°C, especially in Priorat). The Ebro Delta is windy and mosquito-infested in summer.

title: Catalan Pyrenees - 6-Day Discovery
subtitle: 6 days to cross the high Catalan Pyrenees, from La Seu d'Urgell's Romanesque cathedral to the UNESCO churches of the Vall de Boí, reaching the Occitan Aran Valley.
climate: Best time: June to September, temperatures 15-25°C in valleys, cooler at altitude. Avoid October-May: heavy snow, mountain passes closed (especially around Vielha), roads impassable. The Catalan Pyrenees are a high-mountain summer destination.

title: Castile and León - 14-Day Grand Tour
subtitle: Fourteen days exploring the historic heart of Castile, from the Roman aqueduct of Segovia to the Gothic stained glass of León, via golden Salamanca, the glacial lake of Sanabria, and the wine country of Ribera del Duero.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C across the Castilian plateau. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 35-40°C, cities turn into ovens) and December-February (harsh cold, frequent snow at altitude). Cool nights even in summer, significant temperature swings.

title: Catalonia - Complete 24-Day Grand Tour
subtitle: A full 24-day loop from Barcelona, spanning the Ebro Delta to the Pyrenees of Val d'Aran, through Priorat vineyards, Romanesque valleys and the Catalan interior.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 20-26°C on the coast, 15-20°C in the Pyrenees. Avoid July-August (scorching heat in Barcelona 30-35°C, crowds) and December-March (Pyrenean passes closed, snow). Bring windbreaker for high altitudes even in summer.

title: Catalan Pyrenees and Romanesque Art in 8 Days: Lleida, Pallars, Vall de Boí and Val d'Aran
subtitle: 8 days to climb from the Lleida plain to the Atlantic slopes of the Garonne, following the trail of cathedrals and Lombard Romanesque churches in the high valleys.
climate: Best period: June-September, with 15-25°C in the mountains (Vielha, Vall de Boí) and up to 30°C in Lleida. Avoid November-April: snowy passes, high mountain roads closed. Also avoid July-August in Lleida (scorching heat 35-40°C); leave early morning instead.

title: Catalonia Wine Route - 7 Days from Penedès to Priorat
subtitle: Seven days exploring Catalonia's great wine terroirs, from Penedès cava to the mineral reds of Priorat, via the sacred mountain of Montserrat and the salt fortress of Cardona.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 18-26°C, ideal for wine tasting and vineyard walks. Avoid July-August (scorching heat up to 35-38°C in Priorat and Penedès, harvest season). Mild winters on the coast but cold at Montserrat and Cardona.

title: Southern Catalonia in 5 Days: Roman Tarragona, Poblet, Priorat and the Port of Cambrils
subtitle: Five days linking the imperial capital of Hispania with a mountaintop village in Priorat, via the ramparts of Montblanc and the fishing port of Cambrils.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 20-28°C with generous sunshine. Avoid July-August (stifling heat exceeding 35°C on the coast, heavy tourist crowds). Autumn brings occasional Mediterranean showers, especially in October.

title: Galicia: Complete 10-Day Circuit
subtitle: Ten days to loop through Atlantic Galicia, from Santiago's cathedral to La Coruña's Roman lighthouse, through the wine-growing Rías Baixas to the sacred canyons of Ribeira Sacra and Lugo's Roman ramparts.
climate: Best time: May to September, temperatures 18-25°C, mild Atlantic climate. Avoid October-March: heavy, near-daily rainfall (up to 150 rainy days/year on the coast). Cooler summers than the rest of Spain, ideal for avoiding heat.

title: La Rioja in 7 Days - The Kingdom of Tempranillo
subtitle: 7 days between the Sierra de Cantabria and the Ebro, from century-old bodegas to architectures signed by Gehry and Calatrava.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for winery visits. Avoid July-August (intense heat 35-40°C, little shade in vineyards). Cold winters with frequent frost, occasional snow.

title: Ribera del Duero in 7 days: The Plateau That Rivals Rioja
subtitle: A 7 days circuit connecting Madrid to Valladolid along the Golden Mile, through Vega Sicilia, Pingus, Aalto, and 13th-century cave cellars.
keywords: ribera del duero wine route spain
dominio de pingus ribera del duero
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, 15-25°C ideal for cellar visits. Avoid July-August (scorching heat up to 35°C) and December-February (frequent frost on this 800 m plateau). Cool nights year-round, bring a jacket.

title: Navarre Road Trip: Castles, Irati Forest & the Camino
subtitle: From Pamplona to Roncesvalles, explore Gothic palaces, hilltop villages, and Pyrenean beech forests along the Way of St. James.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C. Avoid July-August (scorching heat in Pamplona, up to 35°C) and December-March (frequent snow in Irati Forest, roads sometimes closed). Autumn brings spectacular colors to the Pyrenean beech forests.

title: Dark Andalusia: 10 Days from Seville to Málaga
subtitle: 10 days following the Inquisition pyres, the last Moorish kings weeping for their kingdom, the Civil War dead, and the hooded processions of Holy Week, from Seville to Málaga.
climate: Best time: April-May and October-November, temperatures 18-28°C. Avoid July-August at all costs: Seville and Córdoba regularly exceed 40°C with brutal dry heat. Mild winter (10-16°C) but frequent rain.

title: Mystical Castile: Toledo, Ávila, Salamanca & Burgos in 11 Days
subtitle: 11 days through the lands of saints, witches and Templars, from Madrid to Burgos across the mesetas
keywords: cristo de la luz mosque toledo
climate: Best period: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for sightseeing. Avoid July-August (scorching heat exceeding 35°C on the Castilian plateau) and December-February (harsh cold, frequent frost). Continental highland climate: cool nights even in summer.

title: Costa Blanca & Alicante Hinterland: 12 days
subtitle: An 12 days road trip between vertiginous capes, mountain villages, and pink lagoons.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, with temperatures from 20 to 28°C and excellent sunshine. Avoid July-August when coastal heat can exceed 35°C. The inland areas are 3-5°C cooler, mild winter but chilly at higher elevations.

title: Northern Valencia Road Trip: 11 days Through Medieval Villages
subtitle: From the ramparts of Morella to the beaches of Gandia, 11 days through the Valencian Community at its finest.
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, temperatures 18-26°C, ideal for both inland mountains and coast. Avoid July-August (scorching heat up to 35°C inland, crowded beaches in Peniscola). Watch for potential heavy autumn rains in September-October.

title: Murcia Coast and Hinterland: 13 days from La Manga to Cuenca
subtitle: A 13 days road trip between salt lagoon, lunar badlands, medieval towns, and protected rice paddies.
keywords: Caravaca de la Cruz holy city
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 20-28°C on the coast, cooler in Cuenca (15-22°C). Avoid July-August: scorching heat in Murcia (35-40°C), unbearable during the day. Winter is mild on the coast but cold in Cuenca with possible snow.

title: Al-Andalus: From the Mezquita to the Alpujarras in 10 Days
subtitle: Following the caliphal and Mudéjar trail from Seville to Trevélez over 10 days.
climate: Best period: April-May and October-November, temperatures 18-28°C. Strictly avoid July-August: Seville and Córdoba regularly exceed 40°C, making monument visits grueling. The Alpujarras stay cooler at altitude (15-25°C) but prepare for temperature contrasts with the cities.

title: Costa de la Luz: 7 Days from Seville to Tarifa
subtitle: Raw Atlantic coastline, bluefin tuna, and Roman ruins: Andalusia facing Morocco in 7 days.
keywords: Vejer de la Frontera white village
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 20-28°C on the coast, moderate winds. Avoid July-August: scorching heat in Seville (38-42°C) and strong winds in Tarifa (Levante). Mild winter (15-18°C) but very windy along the Atlantic coast.

title: Wild Coast: Málaga to Cartagena in 9 Days
subtitle: From Frigiliana to Cabo de Gata, 9 days on the Andalusian coast Marbella forgot.
keywords: Málaga to Cartagena road trip by car
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, temperatures 20-26°C, pleasant sea. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 35-40°C, especially in Tabernas desert). Mild winter (15-18°C) but some coastal sites can be windy.

title: Huelva, the Forgotten Land: Doñana to Faro in 7 Days
subtitle: From Seville to the Algarve: 7 days in Andalusia's blind spot.
keywords: Doñana Algarve road trip by car
Palos de la Frontera Christopher Columbus
climate: Best period: March-May and October-November, temperatures 18-28°C, ideal for Doñana and Sierra de Aracena. Avoid July-August (frequent 40°C, scorching heat). Mild but rainy winter, mountain roads occasionally difficult.

title: White Villages: Arcos, Grazalema, Ronda - 6 Days
subtitle: The white villages route in depth, from Seville to Málaga in 6 days.
keywords: Vía Verde de la Sierra cycling
climate: Best time: April-May and October-November, temperatures 18-28°C, ideal for hiking. Avoid July-August (extreme heat 35-42°C, villages and roads become unbearable). Grazalema gets heavy winter rainfall, occasional snow at altitude.

title: White Villages & Sierras: 9 Days Málaga-Almería
subtitle: From the Costa del Sol to the sunburnt badlands of Tabernas, 9 days through Andalusia's white villages and ancient dolmens.
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, temperatures 20-28°C. Avoid July-August: scorching heat inland (35-40°C) and in Tabernas desert. Mild coastal winter but cool in the sierras (Ronda).

title: Campo de Calatrava and Valle de Alcudia in 5 Days
subtitle: From Ciudad Real to Almadén, a road trip through Calatrava knights, Valdepeñas vineyards, and UNESCO mercury mines.
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, comfortable 15-25°C temperatures for exploring. Avoid July-August: scorching heat (35-40°C), parched landscapes. Winter is cold with frequent nighttime frost.

title: La Siberia and La Serena: Secret Extremadura in 6 Days
subtitle: From Don Benito to Herrera del Duque, a road trip through giant lakes, forgotten castles, and millennia-old rock art.
climate: Best time: April-May and October-November, temperatures 15-25°C. Avoid July-August at all costs (extreme heat, 35-42°C). Dry continental climate with cool nights even in summer.

title: Castilla-La Mancha: Windmills and Sierra in 10 Days
subtitle: From Cuenca to Valencia's vineyards in 10 days, between Don Quixote and the Sierra Segura.
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C. Avoid July-August at all costs (scorching heat 35-40°C on the Meseta, bone-dry). Cold winters with frequent frost in higher elevations.

title: Ibiza & Formentera by Scooter: Calas & Beaches in 7 Days
subtitle: A complete tour of the islands' most stunning coves, handlebars in hand, from the wild south to the bohemian north.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 22-28°C, warm sea and fewer crowds. Avoid July-August: intense heat (30-35°C), overcrowded beaches and high prices. Winter is mild but windy, many facilities closed.

title: Finland Grand Tour: 21 Days from Helsinki to Lapland
subtitle: The complete tour of Finland in three weeks: Helsinki, Turku and the archipelago, Åland, flight to Lapland, return via Saimaa lakes and North Karelia.
climate: Best period: June-August, temperatures 15-22°C, long days (midnight sun in Lapland). Avoid October-April: freezing cold (-10 to -30°C in Lapland), polar night, snowy roads. May and September possible but cool (8-15°C).

title: Finnish Lakeland: Saimaa, Medieval Castles & Wild Forests
subtitle: From Helsinki to the lake region by car, five to 6 days between Lake Saimaa, the Punkaharju ridge, and the medieval castle of Savonlinna.
climate: Best period: June-August, temperatures 15-25°C with endless daylight (up to 19 hours). Avoid November-March: severe frost (-10 to -20°C), heavy snow, and very long nights. May and September are viable but cool (5-15°C).

title: North Karelia: Koli's Ancient Peaks and Finland's Wildest Rapids
subtitle: From Joensuu to the Ruunaa rapids via Koli's national landscape—six days in Finland's least-visited east
climate: Best time: June-August (16-22°C) for hiking in Koli, or January-March for cross-country skiing and snowshoeing. Avoid November (long nights, damp cold without snow) and April-May (muddy snowmelt). Harsh winter with -15 to -25°C from December to February.

title: Southeast Finland Circuit: Porvoo Archipelago & National Parks
subtitle: From Vantaa to Kouvola, a week between wooden old towns, Gulf of Finland coastline, and national forests.
climate: Best time: June-August, temperatures 15-22°C with long daylight hours (white nights in June). Avoid November-March: harsh cold (-10 to -5°C), limited daylight. May and September offer good conditions with fewer crowds.

title: Finland Road Trip: From Helsinki to the Northern Lights
subtitle: From Helsinki to Lapland by air—8 days blending Nordic design, industrial heritage, and Arctic nights.
climate: Best time: June-August for midnight sun (15-20°C in the south, 10-15°C in Lapland) or December-March for Northern Lights (-5 to -30°C, guaranteed snow). Avoid November and April: little daylight, icy roads, unpleasant transition. Expect significant temperature differences between Helsinki and Rovaniemi.

title: Southwest Finland: Turku, Archipelago and Åland
subtitle: From Turku to Mariehamn via Northern Europe's densest archipelago – a week between medieval castles, islands and UNESCO heritage.
climate: Best period: June-August, temperatures 15-22°C, long sunny days ideal for exploring the archipelago. Avoid November-March (cold, very short days, most tourist services closed). May and September remain pleasant but cooler (10-15°C).

title: Exploring Saint Barthélemy: From Gustavia Harbor to the Eastern Lagoons
subtitle: Discover the finest bays of Saint Barth, where paradise beaches meet Swedish heritage and untouched nature
climate: Best period: December to April, temperatures 24 to 29°C, little rain and calm seas. Avoid August to October (hurricane season, heavy rains, rough seas). Shoulder season: June-July and November are manageable with brief showers.

title: Ain Road Trip: Bresse Plains and Bugey Mountains
subtitle: From Bresse poultry to Bugey wines, a journey through medieval towns and centuries-old vineyards
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C in the plains, cooler in the Bugey mountains. Avoid winter (dense fog in Bresse, snow in mountains) and July-August (heavy heat, frequent storms). Mountain roads challenging November to March.

title: Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes Road Trip: Monasteries, Mountains & Lakes
subtitle: From Bourg-en-Bresse to the shores of Lake Bourget—a journey through Gothic splendor, Savoyard heritage, and thermal waters.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for lakes and mountains. Avoid winter (snow, alpine roads closed) and July-August (high heat, crowds). Frequent spring rains, bring a raincoat.

title: Aisne Circuit: Gothic Cathedrals and Picardy Nature
subtitle: From cathedral spires to medieval forests, Aisne unfolds its treasures between stone and greenery
climate: Best period: May to September, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for cathedrals and hiking. Avoid November-February (cold, grey, damp, 0-5°C). Spring and autumn often rainy but manageable.

title: Allier Road Trip: Vichy, Thermal Baths and Bourbonnais Bocage
subtitle: From thermal springs to medieval castles, Allier reveals its treasures between wellness and heritage
climate: Best period: May-June and September-October, temperatures 18-24°C, ideal for the bocage countryside and spa towns. Avoid July-August (sometimes heavy heat, up to 30°C) and November-February (cold and damp, 0-5°C, grey skies). Regular rainfall year-round.

title: Alpes-De-Haute-Provence Circuit: Lavender and Perched Villages
subtitle: From the Verdon gorges to lavender fields, a journey between vertiginous canyons and fragrant plateaus
climate: Best time: May-June (lavender blooms) and September, temperatures 18-28°C depending on altitude. Avoid July-August (intense heat in valleys, tourist crowds). Harsh winters with mountain roads sometimes closed.

title: Forcalquier & Verdon Gorges Road Trip
subtitle: From Vauban citadels to lavender fields — 16 stops between alpine valleys and secret Provence
keywords: road trip alpes de haute provence
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 20-28°C, ideal for hiking the gorges and exploring villages. Avoid July-August (intense heat 30-35°C, crowds) and winter in higher areas (closed mountain passes, snow). The mistral wind can blow strong in spring.

title: Ardèche Road Trip: Wild Gorges and Heritage Villages
subtitle: From Pont d'Arc to perched villages, an odyssey through limestone canyons and Ardèche's timeless heritage
climate: Best period: May-June and September-early October, temperatures 20-28°C, ideal for gorges and villages. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 32-38°C, drought, peak crowds). Watch for violent summer storms and flash floods in the gorges.

title: The 18 Most Beautiful Villages of Ardèche
subtitle: An 18-stage journey through Ardèche's heritage villages, from Pont d'Arc to the Doux gorges, weaving between limestone cliffs, medieval castles and basalt stone.
climate: Best period: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for exploring villages on foot. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 30-35°C, drought, tourist crowds). Watch for violent summer thunderstorms in the gorges and strong Mistral winds.

title: Ardennes Road Trip: Forests & Fortresses (3 Days)
subtitle: From Vauban's ramparts to the winding Meuse, a military and natural odyssey through the heart of Europe
climate: Best time: May-September, temperatures 15-23°C, ideal for forest hiking. Avoid November-March: cold and damp, frequent fog in valleys. Rain possible year-round, pack appropriate clothing.

title: Ariège Circuit: Cathar Castles, Prehistoric Caves & Mountain Lakes
subtitle: Journey through Cathar fortresses and underground wonders in the heart of the Ariège Pyrenees
climate: Best period: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for castles and hiking. Avoid winter (mountain roads closed, snow) and July-August (frequent storms at altitude, high crowds). Spring can be rainy until mid-April.

title: Aube Circuit: Champagne, Medieval Heritage and Orient Forest Lakes
subtitle: Between Gothic cathedrals and champagne vineyards, a journey through the heart of Aube
climate: Best time: May-June and September-early October, temperatures 15-24°C, ideal for cellars and heritage sites. Avoid November-March (cold, damp, frequent grey skies, 0-8°C). Pleasant summer but possible storms around the lakes.

title: Aude Road Trip: Carcassonne, Cathar Country & the Mediterranean
subtitle: 7 days among medieval fortresses, charming villages and wild coastline. From Carcassonne to the beaches of Leucate, via the mysteries of Rennes-le-Château.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C. Avoid July-August (intense heat up to 35-40°C, strong tramontane winds on the coast). Mild winters in lowlands but cold in Montagne Noire.

title: Aveyron Road Trip: Causses, Tarn Gorges and Villages
subtitle: From the Soulages Museum to the Roquefort caves, between Romanesque heritage and causse landscapes
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal light for causses and gorges. Avoid November-March (harsh cold on Aubrac plateau, snow risk at Laguiole, fog). July-August: hot but bearable due to altitude, tourist crowds.

title: Alpilles, Aix-en-Provence & Camargue Circuit
subtitle: From the Blue Coast to the banks of the Rhône — 10 days exploring stone-built villages of the Alpilles, the Aix countryside, and the plains of the Camargue.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 18-28°C, ideal light. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 30-35°C, crowds) and mistral periods (violent cold wind). Mild winter (10-15°C) but windy.

title: Calvados Circuit: D-Day Beaches and Normandy
subtitle: From the cliffs of Omaha to the boardwalks of Deauville, a Normandy between remembrance and elegance
climate: Best period: May-September, temperatures 15-22°C, ideal for coastal visits and historic sites. Avoid November-February (frequent rain, grey skies, windy beaches). Oceanic climate: bring a windbreaker even in summer, changeable weather.

title: Cantal Road Trip: Auvergne Volcanoes and Mountain Traditions
subtitle: From the peaks of Europe's largest stratovolcano to bubbling hot springs, a Cantal between mineral majesty and pastoral gentleness
climate: Best time: May to September, temperatures 15-25°C in valleys (cooler at altitude). Avoid November-March: snow, difficult mountain roads and closed passes. Bring warm clothing even in summer for higher elevations.

title: Auvergne's Volcanic Villages Trail
subtitle: From the infant Loire to the Cantal highlands: 8 stages blending medieval basalt architecture with volcanic landscapes
climate: Best time: May-June and September, temperatures 15-23°C, ideal for hilltop villages. Avoid November-March (harsh cold, frequent snow at altitude, roads sometimes tricky). July-August: decent weather but tourist crowds.

title: Charente Circuit: Cognac and Romanesque Heritage in 4 Days
subtitle: From centuries-old cellars to Romanesque churches, the Charente between vineyards and river
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, 15-25°C, ideal for castle tours and cognac tastings. Avoid November-March (grey, rainy, damp cold). July-August: hot (28-32°C) but manageable, perfect for riverside terraces.

title: Charente-Maritime Road Trip: La Rochelle, Île de Ré, Fort Boyard and Roman Saintonge
subtitle: From La Rochelle's medieval towers to Vauban citadels, between luminous islands and wild estuary
climate: Best period: May-June and September-early October, temperatures 18-24°C, optimal sunshine and fewer crowds. Avoid July-August if you want to skip the massive tourist influx on the islands (Ré, Oléron), though temperatures remain pleasant (22-28°C). Mild but rainy and windy winters, not recommended for coastal visits.

climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 20-27°C, ideal for gorges and lake activities. Avoid July-August (scorching heat up to 35°C, massive crowds, parking nightmare). Mistral winds can blow strong year-round.

title: The 15 Most Beautiful Villages of the Luberon
subtitle: From village to village between limestone ridges, blazing ochres and lavender fields: a complete tour of the Luberon's and Vaucluse Mountains' perched jewels.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-28°C, golden light on the villages. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 30-35°C+, massive tourist crowds). The mistral wind can blow strongly year-round, bring a windbreaker.

title: Vaucluse: Avignon, Luberon Villages & Ventoux Routes
subtitle: From papal palaces to ochre cliffs, 6 days among vineyards, hilltop villages, and mysterious springs
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, 18-28°C, ideal light. Avoid July-August (intense heat 32-38°C, violent mistral wind) and winter on Ventoux (closed roads). Bring windproof jacket: the mistral blows 100+ days a year.

title: Vendée Tour: Noirmoutier Island, Puy du Fou & Coast
subtitle: From Atlantic islands to medieval fortresses, discover Vendée's maritime soul and bocage charm
climate: Best time: May to September, temperatures 18-25°C, ideal for the coast and Puy du Fou. Avoid November-March (grey weather, Atlantic cold, strong winds on the islands). Summer stays mild, rarely above 28°C.

title: Vienne Road Trip: Futuroscope and Romanesque Heritage
subtitle: From Poitiers to Richelieu, a journey through thousand-year-old frescoes, medieval fortresses, and the Cardinal's ideal city
climate: Best period: May-June and September-October, temperatures 18-25°C, pleasant sunshine. Avoid November-March (damp cold, frequent rain, 5-10°C). Summer warm but manageable (25-28°C), occasional thunderstorms.

title: Haute-Vienne Circuit: Limoges, Porcelain & Historic Villages
subtitle: Journey through centuries of craftsmanship, from world-renowned porcelain workshops to medieval abbeys and martyred villages
climate: Best time: May-September, temperatures 18-25°C, ideal for exploring Limoges and villages. Avoid November-March: cold, damp weather (0-8°C), often overcast. Rainfall year-round, bring an umbrella even in summer.

title: Haute-Vienne Road Trip: Medieval Castles & the Périgord-Limousin
subtitle: From medieval fortresses to gold-mining villages, a journey through the heart of the Limousin—where Plantagenet history, extraordinary geology, and living memory converge.
climate: Best time: May-June and September, 15-25°C, perfect for castles and countryside drives. Avoid November-February (constant rain, overcast, 3-8°C). Summer is pleasant but expect afternoon thunderstorms.

title: Vosges Abbey Trail: Monastic Heritage Unveiled
subtitle: From Lorraine's monastic cross to forgotten southern sanctuaries — 5 days tracing Benedictine, Cistercian and Premonstratensian footsteps
climate: Best period: May to September, temperatures 15-25°C in valleys. Avoid December-February: snowy mountain roads, Étanche Abbey hard to reach. Cool but lush spring in the Vosges massif.

title: Yonne Circuit: Northern Burgundy & Chablis Wine Country
subtitle: Where Gothic cathedrals rise above legendary vineyards and medieval pilgrimage routes
climate: Best period: May-June and September-early October, temperatures 15-23°C, ideal for Chablis vineyards and medieval villages. Avoid July-August (heat and tourist crowds) and November-March (cold damp weather, 2-8°C, frequent grey skies). September offers grape harvest and autumn colors.

title: Territoire de Belfort Road Trip: The Citadel and the Lion
subtitle: From Vauban's mighty fortifications to the peaks of the Vosges, the Territoire de Belfort blends the heroic resistance of 1870 with trails through the Ballon d'Alsace.

title: Essonne Journey: Castles and River Valleys
subtitle: Discover medieval fortresses, Renaissance gardens, and artistic heritage in the heart of Île-de-France
climate: Best period: May-June and September, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for visiting castles and gardens. Avoid July-August (heat and frequent storms) and December-February (grey, rainy, 3-8°C). Spring perfect for Essonne's parks and forests.

title: Hauts-de-Seine Road Trip: Royal Heritage & Gardens
subtitle: Discover Napoleon's legacy and royal gardens in the heart of Île-de-France
climate: Best period: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-23°C, ideal for gardens and urban visits. Avoid July-August (oppressive urban heat, up to 35°C) and November-February (cold, wet, short days). Rain spread throughout the year, bring an umbrella.

title: Seine-Saint-Denis Circuit: Royal Basilica and Urban Culture
subtitle: From the gothic splendor of France's royal necropolis to the vibrant street art of industrial suburbs
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-22°C, ideal for visiting the Basilica and cultural sites. Avoid July-August (urban heat up to 30°C, heavy tourist crowds) and December-February (cold damp weather, grey skies). Moderate rainfall year-round.

title: Val-de-Marne Road Trip: Castles and River Marne Banks
subtitle: From Vincennes to Charenton, a complete tour of Val-de-Marne between medieval fortresses, Marne riverbanks and remarkable gardens.
climate: Best period: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-23°C, ideal for castles and riverside walks along the Marne. Avoid July-August (frequent heatwaves up to 35°C, stifling air) and December-February (grey, damp, 2-8°C). Parisian oceanic climate: rain possible year-round.

title: Val-d'Oise Circuit: Impressionist Villages and Heritage
subtitle: From Cergy to La Roche-Guyon, 3 days through the Oise Valley, medieval abbeys, and Impressionist villages.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 12-22°C, ideal for exploring Impressionist villages and gardens. Avoid winter (grey, damp, 2-8°C) and July-August (occasional heatwaves, thunderstorms). Moderate rainfall year-round, bring an umbrella.

title: Grand Tour of the French Alps: From the Thousand Ponds to Mont Blanc
subtitle: From the bird-filled ponds of the Dombes to the eternal snows of Mont Blanc, an unforgettable Alpine odyssey
climate: Best period: May-June and September, with temperatures of 15-25°C in valleys and lake areas. Avoid December-March (Alpine passes closed, ski resorts crowded) and July-August (heat in the Ain lowlands, heavy crowds on the lakes). Chamonix heights remain cool even in summer.

title: Alsace's Most Beautiful Christmas Markets
subtitle: From Mulhouse to Strasbourg: 7 days of Advent magic through medieval villages and festive traditions
climate: Best time: late November to late December for Christmas markets, daytime temperatures 0-5°C (often below freezing at night). Bring warm, waterproof clothing: rain, snow and fog are common. Avoid January-February if you're after the festive atmosphere.

title: Lyon to Annecy Road Trip: Heritage and Alpine Lakes
subtitle: 4 days from gastronomic capital to medieval town, Savoyard spa resorts and alpine lakes

climate: Best period: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for Alpine lakes. Avoid July-August (heatwaves in Lyon up to 35°C, heavy crowds in Annecy) and December-February (snow on mountain roads). Afternoon thunderstorms common in summer near the Alps.

subtitle: From Chamonix to Nyons in 5 days, between alpine peaks, Lyon's heritage, and Provençal scents.

title: Burgundy's 15 Most Beautiful Villages
subtitle: From Chablis to Paray-le-Monial, a road trip through classified vineyards, thousand-year abbeys, hilltop villages, and Burgundian Romanesque art
climate: Best period: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for village visits. Avoid July-August (heat often exceeding 30°C, peak tourist crowds) and December-February (frost, frequent valley fog). Moderate rainfall year-round.

title: Burgundy Road Trip: Cathedrals, Vineyards & Medieval Villages
subtitle: From Sens to Dijon via Vézelay, Beaune, and the most beautiful villages of Yonne and Côte-d'Or
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C ideal for vineyards and villages. Avoid July-August (intense heat up to 35°C) and December-February (cold, frequent frost, many cellars closed). Moderate rainfall year-round.

title: Brittany Road Trip: Historic Villages, Sea and Heritage
subtitle: Discover 18 of Brittany's most beautiful coastal villages, from medieval ramparts to pink granite chaos
climate: Best time: May-June and September, temperatures 15-22°C, optimal sunshine. Avoid November-March (constant rain, strong coastal winds). Summer is crowded with unpredictable weather.

subtitle: Journey through Renaissance splendor where royal dreams rise from the waters of France's garden

subtitle: 13 days circling the Isle of Beauty: red porphyry cliffs, perched Genoese villages, laricio pine forests, and white sand beaches from Porto-Vecchio to Bonifacio.

subtitle: 4 days between the Phocaean city, Cassis calanques, the birthplace of cinema in La Ciotat, Le Castellet vineyards, and Aix's elegance.

title: The Great Museums of Paris
subtitle: A complete tour of Paris's 21 essential museums, from the medieval world of Cluny to the avant-garde at Beaubourg, passing through the Water Lilies at the Orangerie and the world cultures at Quai Branly.
cities: musee d art moderne de paris
cite de l architecture et du patrimoine
cite des sciences et de l industrie
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October (15-25°C), ideal for museum visits without summer crowds. Avoid July-August (intense heat up to 35°C, tourist rush) and rainy winter. Bring an umbrella year-round.

title: France's Most Beautiful Villages: Alps to Luberon in 3 Days
subtitle: From Saint-Véran at 6,699 feet to Roussillon's ochres and Gordes' golden stone - seven villages between Alpine peaks, Verdon gorges, and Luberon lavender

subtitle: 3 days between semi-wild bison, Vauban citadels, a turquoise canyon, and faience villages, from the Grasse Pre-Alps to the Valensole plateau.

title: Grand Est Road Trip: Vineyards, Ramparts and Forests
subtitle: A journey through fortified towns, spa heritage, and wine villages that capture the soul of Eastern France
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, perfect for Alsace vineyards. Avoid January-February (frequent frost in the Vosges, below freezing). July-August pleasant but sometimes hot (28-32°C) with occasional thunderstorms.

title: Nouvelle-Aquitaine Loop: Medieval Villages, Vineyards & Dordogne Valley
subtitle: From the Basque Country to Île de Ré via Saint-Émilion, the Dordogne Valley, and Périgord

climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 18-25°C, ideal for the Basque coast and Périgord. Avoid July-August (intense heat inland, 30-35°C, heavy tourist crowds). Mild autumn but expect Atlantic rain on La Rochelle and Biarritz.

title: Normandy Coastal Road Trip: Cliffs, Harbors & Mont-Saint-Michel
subtitle: From the chalk cliffs of Étretat to the ramparts of Mont-Saint-Michel, 12 days on the road along Normandy's coastline—between D-Day memory, fishing harbors and seaside towns.

title: Complete Normandy Road Trip: Cliffs, D-Day Beaches and Mont-Saint-Michel
subtitle: From Bayeux to Caen in 7 days, between D-Day memory, wild Cotentin coast, Orne valleys and Alabaster Coast cliffs.
climate: Best time: May to September, temperatures 18-24°C. Visit June-August for beaches, but always pack a windbreaker along the coast. Avoid October-March: frequent rain, strong winds, cool weather (5-10°C).

title: Normandy's Bay of Mont-Saint-Michel: Coastal Drives & the Sacred Abbey
climate: Best period: May to September, temperatures 15-22°C, ideal for spring tides and coastal visits. Avoid November-March: frequent rain, strong winds and cold (5-10°C). Ocean winds can blow year-round, bring a windbreaker even in summer.

title: Occitanie's 13 Most Beautiful Villages
subtitle: From the bastides of the Tarn to the gorges of the Tarn River, thirteen medieval stops through southern France
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October (15-25°C) for comfortable sightseeing. Avoid July-August: scorching heat (30-35°C) and heavy tourist crowds. Pyrenean villages are cooler; mountain passes closed November-April.

title: Midi-Pyrénées Circuit: Bastides, Cliffs and Abbeys
subtitle: From Cordes-sur-Ciel to Conques, 2 days among medieval bastides, perched villages above the Lot and Romanesque sanctuaries of the Rouergue.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for exploring hilltop villages. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 30-35°C in the gorges) and December-February (cliff roads slippery in freezing conditions).

title: Paris to Bordeaux Road Trip: Loire Valley Châteaux and Vineyards
subtitle: From the Eiffel Tower to Saint-Émilion vineyards, 8 days across France: monumental Paris, Norman coast and D-Day beaches, Renaissance châteaux of the Loire Valley, classic Bordeaux and the Arcachon Basin.
climate: Visit from May to June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for châteaux and vineyards. Avoid July-August (peak crowds, heavy heat in Bordeaux) and November-March (frequent rain, grey skies in Normandy).

title: Southeast France Road Trip: Perched Villages and Dramatic Gorges
subtitle: From Auvillar to Hyères in 7 days, between Quercy bastides, Aveyron causses, Hérault gorges, Roman arenas, and Verdon calanques.

title: Bordeaux to Toulouse Road Trip: Ocean, Pyrenees and Heritage
subtitle: 6 days through Bordeaux vineyards, the Dune du Pilat, Landes surf beaches, Basque villages, the hot springs of Dax and the pink-brick streets of Toulouse.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 18-26°C, optimal sunshine and fewer crowds. Avoid July-August on the Basque coast (overcrowding, high prices) and in Toulouse (intense heat 32-36°C). Autumn and winter bring heavy Atlantic rains, mountain roads occasionally closed in winter.

title: The Grand Tour of France in 5 Weeks, Car-Free
subtitle: This itinerary takes you through the most beautiful places in France — the world's most visited country — without ever getting behind a wheel. Culture, heritage, landscapes and coastlines: the road trip of a lifetime.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-early October, with temperatures between 15-25°C across most of the route. Avoid July-August: scorching heat in the South (35-40°C in Marseille, Avignon), tourist crowds, inflated prices. Northern and western regions remain rainy year-round, pack a raincoat.

title: Bordeaux in 7 Days: Médoc, Saint-Émilion, and Sauternes
subtitle: A loop circuit through the world's most prestigious vineyards, from the powerful Médoc to the golden sweet wines of Sauternes via the finesse of the Right Bank.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 18-25°C, perfect for wine tours. Avoid November-March (frequent rain, grey vineyards). July-August: hot but dry, up to 30°C.

title: Loire Valley Wine Route in 8 days
subtitle: An 800-kilometer route connects Sancerre to Nantes through 68 appellations, troglodyte caves, and Renaissance châteaux along the UNESCO-listed river.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for cellar visits and vineyard walks. Avoid July-August (tourist crowds, heat) and November-March (dormant vines, frequent rain, cold). September offers the harvest atmosphere.

title: Burgundy Grand Crus Route - 7 Days from Chablis to the Mâconnais
subtitle: A 7 days itinerary covering 220 km of UNESCO-listed vineyards, from Chablis Chardonnay to Mâconnais Pouilly-Fuissé.
keywords: côte de nuits côte de beaune wine route
climate: Best period: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for wine tasting. Avoid July-August (intense heat up to 35°C in vineyards) and November-March (cold, damp, many cellars closed). September offers the grape harvest experience.

title: Rhône Valley : Northern Syrahs to Southern Grenaches
subtitle: A 200 km route along the Rhône that crosses two opposing wine worlds, from the vertical granite terraces of Côte-Rôtie to the sun-baked rolled pebbles of Châteauneuf-du-Pape.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-28°C, ideal for vineyard tours. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 35-40°C in the valley, strong mistral winds possible). Fall perfect for harvest season, mild winter but frequent mistral.

title: Haunted Île-de-France: 5 Days of Royal Ghosts and Revolutionary Prisons
subtitle: A five-day loop from Paris through Marie-Antoinette's last cell, the Trianon time-slip affair, the tallest medieval keep in Europe, and six million Parisian skeletons stored underground.
climate: Best period: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-22°C, ideal for visiting castles and monuments. Avoid December-February (grey, rainy, cold around 5°C) and occasional summer heatwaves (30°C+). Indoor visits remain viable year-round.

title: Haunted Brittany in 6 days: From Brocéliande to the Lands of the Ankou
subtitle: A 6 days circuit crossing Brittany from Rennes to Quimper, through the Arthurian forests of Brocéliande to the peat bog gates of hell in the Monts d'Arrée and the fortress lair of a Renaissance warlord.
keywords: pointe du raz bay of the dead
climate: Best time: May-September, 16-22°C, ideal for forests and coast. Avoid November-February (frequent rain, overcast, strong winds). Even in summer, bring a raincoat.

title: Haunted Provence - 5 Days Through Cursed Castles, Asylums, and Ancien Régime Crimes
subtitle: A 5-day circuit from Avignon to Aix-en-Provence traces the Midi's most documented criminal affairs—from prisoners thrown from Les Baux to Van Gogh's 53 weeks in a still-operating asylum.
keywords: chateau des baux de provence history
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, temperatures 18-25°C, ideal light. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 30-38°C, frequent heatwaves). Beware of the mistral in winter—icy wind despite mild temperatures.

title: Alsace Wine Route in 7 Days
subtitle: A 170 km route at the foot of the Vosges links Strasbourg to Mulhouse through half-timbered villages, seven grape varieties, and grands crus renowned worldwide.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, 15-25°C, perfect for harvest season and winery visits. Avoid July-August (intense heat, up to 32°C) and December-February (cold, grey skies). Semi-continental climate sheltered by the Vosges mountains.

title: Haunted Southwest - 5 Days from the Black Périgord to Cathar Castles
subtitle: A 5-day circuit from Sarlat to Carcassonne traces the sites of Southern France's best-documented dramas - Templar prison, pyre of 200 Cathars at the foot of Montségur, Inquisition established since 1234 in the cellars of the comtal castle.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for visiting castles and villages. Avoid July-August: scorching heat in Carcassonne (up to 35°C), crowded sites. Mild but very rainy winters in Dordogne and Ariège.

title: South Corsica Circuit: Bonifacio, Porto-Vecchio and Mountain Ranges
subtitle: Where turquoise Mediterranean waters meet soaring Corsican peaks—explore Napoleon's imperial city of Ajaccio, Bonifacio's dramatic limestone cliffs, Porto-Vecchio's paradise beaches, and Piana's UNESCO-listed red porphyry calanques
climate: Best period: May-June and September-October, temperatures 22-28°C, warm sea, fewer crowds. Avoid July-August (intense heat 30-35°C, overcrowded beaches, high prices). The mountainous interior (Zonza) stays cool even in summer.

title: Finistère Circuit: Brittany's Wild Edge, Islands & Artists' Villages
subtitle: From Ouessant's timeless lighthouses to Concarneau's walled citadel, a journey through jagged cliffs, sentinel islands, and artists' havens at land's end.
climate: Best time: May to September, temperatures 16-22°C with decent sunshine. Avoid November-February (persistent rain, strong winds, constant gray skies). Finistère stays windy even in summer, bring windproof clothing.

title: Haute-Saône Circuit: Comtois Plateaus and Heritage
subtitle: Discover ancient abbeys, Roman thermal baths, and Le Corbusier's UNESCO masterpiece in eastern France's hidden gem
climate: Best time: May-June and September, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for heritage sites and countryside. Avoid December-February (cold, gray skies, snowy plateau roads) and July-August (heavy heat, frequent storms). Typical semi-continental Franche-Comté climate.

title: Pyrenean Thermal Springs Route: Mountain Spa Journey
subtitle: 5 days of healing waters and mountain grandeur from the Louron Valley to the Azun Valley, discovering thermal spa complexes and the natural wonders of the Hautes-Pyrénées.
keywords: Pic du Midi de Bigorre cable car
climate: Best period: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, mountain passes open. Avoid December-March (snow, closed roads). July-August: frequent afternoon thunderstorms at altitude.

title: Opal Coast Villages and Flemish Heartlands
subtitle: Where medieval ramparts meet windswept beaches and northern light transforms every view
climate: Best time: May to September, temperatures 16-22°C, ideal for coast and villages. Avoid November-February (grey skies, frequent rain, icy winds on the coast). Bring a windbreaker year-round on the Opal Coast.

title: Hérault Road Trip: Montpellier, Beaches and the Hinterland
subtitle: From Montpellier to the Mourèze cirque: between the blue lagoon, Molière's village and the red earth of Salagou.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-early October, temperatures 18-26°C, optimal sunshine. Avoid July-August (intense heat 30-35°C, tourist crowds) and late September-October (risk of violent Cévennes storms in the hinterland). Mild but windy winters (tramontane).

title: Ille-et-Vilaine Circuit: Saint-Malo, Dinard & the Emerald Coast
subtitle: From the corsair city to medieval fortresses, a Brittany of granite facing the ocean
climate: Best time: May-June and September, temperatures 16-22°C, less rain. Avoid November-March (very rainy, grey, frequent wind on the coast). In Saint-Malo, spectacular spring tides but check times for rampart visits.

title: Indre Road Trip: Secret Berry and the Vallée Noire
subtitle: From Talleyrand's châteaux to George Sand's villages, journey through intimate Berry between medieval valleys and literary landscapes
climate: Best period: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for heritage sites and nature. Avoid November-March (cold, grey, frequent rain). July-August can be warm (up to 30°C) but remain bearable in this rural countryside.

title: Jura Road Trip: Lakes, Waterfalls & Mountains
subtitle: From Arbois wine cellars to Salins underground galleries, from Château-Chalon cliffs to Chalain's turquoise waters: the Jura unfolds its terroirs, UNESCO heritage, and dramatic limestone cirques.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, waterfalls at their peak after snowmelt. Avoid winter (December-March): snowy roads, closed sites. Summer (July-August) remains mild but frequent afternoon thunderstorms.

title: Nièvre Road Trip: Morvan Forests and Ducal Heritage
subtitle: From Nevers to Decize, between the Loire River and the Morvan woodlands
climate: Best time: May-June and September, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for the Morvan hills. Avoid November-February (cold, damp, thick fog in the massif, 2-8°C). Frequent rainfall year-round, bring a windbreaker.

title: The 20 Most Beautiful Villages in Normandy
subtitle: From chalk cliffs to secret valleys and bocage countryside: a journey through Normandy's most enchanting villages
climate: Best time: May to September, temperatures 15-22°C, ideal for coastal and countryside villages. Avoid November-February (frequent rain, grey skies, damp cold). Bring a windbreaker even in summer: sea wind is constant along the Norman coast.

title: Jura Lakes Route: Emerald and Turquoise Waters
subtitle: 2 days among glacial lakes, waterfalls and Jura forests, from Chalain to Thoirette along the emerald waters of the "lake district".
climate: Best time: May to September, temperatures 18-28°C, ideal for enjoying the lakes. Avoid November-March: harsh cold, heavy snow, difficult mountain roads. The Jura has a mountain climate with regular rainfall even in summer.

title: Somme Road Trip: Bay of Somme and War Memorials
subtitle: From France's grandest cathedral to a sweeping maritime estuary, journey through WWI battlefields and along the shores of the Bay of Somme.
climate: Best time: May to September, temperatures 15-22°C, ideal for the Bay of Somme. Avoid November-March (cold, rain, icy coastal winds). Bring a windbreaker even in summer, weather changes quickly.

title: Provence & French Riviera Road Trip: Lavender, Hilltop Villages & Beaches
subtitle: From the azure waters of Nice to the papacy palaces of Avignon, discover southern France's most enchanting landscapes
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 18-26°C, ideal for exploring villages and coast. Avoid July-August: scorching heat (30-35°C), massive crowds and doubled prices. Watch for the mistral (cold wind) which can blow year-round, especially inland Provence.

title: Vosges Road Trip: Lakes, Mountains & Thermal Spas
subtitle: From Épinal prints to the Hohneck summit, a journey through centuries-old spa towns, glacial lakes, and fir forests in the heart of the Vosges massif.
climate: Best period: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for hiking and lakes. Avoid December-March (difficult mountain roads, frequent snow, quieter thermal spas). July-August more crowded, possible afternoon storms.

title: Ariège Road Trip: Cathar Castles and Prehistoric Caves
subtitle: From Niaux's painted caves to Cathar citadels, between Pyrenean spas and the episcopal towns of Couserans.
climate: Best period: May-June and September, temperatures 15-25°C in valleys, cooler at altitude. Avoid December-March (frequent snow, closed mountain passes, challenging roads) and July-August (tourist crowds, afternoon mountain thunderstorms). Pyrenean climate is unpredictable: pack warm clothing even in summer for high-altitude sites like Montségur.

title: Hautes-Alpes Road Trip: High Mountains and Alpine Valleys
subtitle: From the turquoise waters of Lake Serre-Ponçon to Briançon's Vauban ramparts, a journey from the Champsaur to the Italian border.
climate: Best time: June to September, with temperatures of 20-28°C in valleys and 15-22°C at altitude. Avoid October to May: many mountain passes closed by snow, high-altitude roads impassable. Exceptional sunshine year-round but frequent afternoon thunderstorms in summer.

title: Alpes-Maritimes Road Trip: French Riviera & Mercantour 13 days
subtitle: From Nice to Cannes in 13 days: baroque palaces, legendary villas, Bronze Age engravings and the perfumes of Grasse.

title: Secret valleys of the Nice hinterland in 6 days
