title: Thrace Road Trip: Kavala, Thasos & Philippi
subtitle: From the Ottoman fortress of Kavala to the megalithic dragon houses of Thasos, through Europe's first Christian site and the colourful alleyways of Xanthi.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 20-28°C, pleasant sea. Avoid July-August (intense heat 35-40°C, crowded beaches) and December-February (frequent rains). Thasos benefits from cooling sea breezes.

title: Northern Greece's Finest: Thessaloniki to Thrace
subtitle: From Thessaloniki to the wild beaches of Halkidiki, from Mount Athos to the Macedonian tombs of Amphipolis: a circuit between the Aegean, ancient history and monastic serenity.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 20-28°C, pleasant sea. Avoid July-August (intense heat in Thessaloniki 35°C+, crowded beaches). Mild coastal winters but rainy.

title: Classical Greece Road Trip: Athens To Olympia
subtitle: From the Acropolis of Athens to the stadiums of Olympia, through the Mycenaean citadels, the Byzantine frescoes of Mystras and the Oracle of Delphi — 8 days across the founding sites of Western civilization.
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, temperatures 18-26°C, ideal for archaeological sites. Avoid July-August: scorching heat (35-40°C), exposed sites unbearable. Arachova at altitude stays cooler; possible snow in winter.

title: Peloponnese Road Trip: Mycenae, Epidaurus & Olympia
subtitle: From the Cyclopean walls of Mycenae to the stone tiers of Epidaurus — where acoustics still baffle engineers — and on to the stadium where Phidias carved the statue of Zeus: the classical Peloponnese in 4 days.
climate: Best period: April-June and September-October, temperatures 20-28°C, ideal for archaeological sites. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 35-40°C, little shade at ruins). Mild but rainy winter, not recommended for outdoor visits.

title: Laconia Road Trip: Sparta, Mystras & Monemvasia
subtitle: From Kalamata's ancient olive groves to the medieval cliff-top lanes of Monemvasia — a journey through Byzantine splendour, UNESCO ruins and fortress-crowned rock.
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, temperatures 20-26°C. Avoid July-August: frequent heatwaves (35-40°C), archaeological sites exposed to full sun. Mild winters (12-15°C) but rainy.

title: Western Peloponnese: Olympia, Three Venetian Fortresses and the Wild Mani
subtitle: An 7 days road trip from the port of Patras to Areopoli, through the birthplace of the Olympic Games, a coast lined with Venetian fortresses, and the Mani peninsula — Greece at its most rugged and most beautiful.
climate: Best period: April-May and September-October, temperatures 20-26°C, swimmable sea. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 35-40°C, very dry) and November-March (frequent rain). The Mani remains arid year-round.

title: Sporades Island Hop: Skiathos, Skopelos & Alonissos
subtitle: From the pine-fringed beaches of Skiathos to the Mamma Mia chapel on Skopelos, and on to the marine park of Alonissos where the last Mediterranean monk seals survive — 6 days across the unspoilt Sporades.
climate: Best period: May-June and September-October, temperatures 22-28°C, pleasant sea. Avoid July-August (intense heat 30-35°C, crowds) and November-March (frequent rain, closures). Refreshing meltemi winds in summer.

title: Thessaly & Meteora
subtitle: From Volos and the Pelion peninsula to the cliff-hanging monasteries of Meteora, this 6 days circuit crosses Thessaly from the Aegean coast to the inland plains — through stone villages, towering rock pillars, and a hidden lake.
climate: Best period: April-May and September-October, temperatures 18-26°C, ideal for visiting the Meteora. Avoid July-August: scorching heat in the Thessalian plain (35-40°C) and Kalambaka makes monastery visits exhausting. Winter can bring cold and snow to Meteora.

title: Epirus & Zagori: Gorges, Stone Villages and the River of the Underworld
subtitle: An 6 days journey through the rugged heart of Epirus, from the Ottoman citadel of Ioannina to the 46 stone villages of Zagori, the world's deepest canyon and the mythological River Acheron.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, 15 to 25°C in the mountains, ideal for hiking. Avoid July-August (scorching heat in Ioannina, up to 35°C) and November-April (snow in Zagori, mountain roads closed or hazardous). Spring: spectacular wildflowers in the gorges.

title: Road Trip Greece: Epirus, Peloponnese and Athens
subtitle: From the wild Ionian coast to the ruins of Delphi, then on to the hills of Athens and the mountains of the Peloponnese before catching the ferry to Italy.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 20-28°C, ideal for archaeological sites and coastal areas. Avoid July-August (intense heat 30-38°C, crowded sites). Note: Delphi and Kalavryta are at altitude and can be cold in winter with occasional snow.

title: Guatemala Grand Tour: 18 Days From South to North
subtitle: Antigua and its volcanoes, the Tz'utujil Maya shores of Lake Atitlán, the Caribbean gorges of the Río Dulce, Garífuna Livingston, the turquoise pools of Semuc Champey, Tikal and the Petén jungle — the complete Guatemala in 18 days.
keywords: guatemala road trip south to north
climate: Best period: November to April (dry season), 64-77°F in the highlands (Antigua, Panajachel), 77-90°F in Petén and the Caribbean coast. Avoid May to October: heavy daily afternoon rains, oppressive humidity, muddy roads to Semuc Champey and Lanquín. September-October especially discouraged (monsoon peak).

title: Wild Eastern Guatemala: Río Dulce, Livingston & Alta Verapaz
subtitle: From Caribbean gorges to turquoise jungle pools: eastern Guatemala far from the beaten path
climate: Best time: December-April, dry season with 25-30°C on the Caribbean coast and 18-22°C in Cobán. Avoid May-October (daily torrential rains, Río Dulce often impassable). Alta Verapaz stays misty and cool year-round, bring a jacket.

title: Northern Guatemala Road Trip: Mayan Jungle, Ancient Temples & Hidden Rivers
subtitle: From Flores to Cobán — seven days of ancient pyramids, underground caves and turquoise pools in the heart of the tropical forest
climate: Best time: November to April (dry season), 20-30°C in the highlands, 25-35°C in Petén. Avoid May-October: daily torrential rains make roads to Semuc Champey/Lanquin very challenging. Flores stays hot and humid year-round, Cobán cooler at altitude.

title: Guatemala Road Trip: Classic South — Antigua, Volcanoes & Lake Atitlán
subtitle: From Guatemala City to Antigua, from Pacaya volcano to Lake Atitlán: 8 days through the best of colonial and indigenous Guatemala.
climate: Best time: November-April (dry season), temperatures 15-25°C thanks to altitude. Avoid May-October: daily afternoon rains, slippery roads to volcanoes and lake. Cool nights year-round in Antigua.

title: Guatemala Belize Road Trip 13 days: Maya & Caribbean
subtitle: Road trip format, different accommodation almost every night, from Antigua's volcanoes to Caye Caulker's reefs in 13 days.

title: Continental Croatia Road Trip: Zagreb, Slavonia and Baroque Castles
subtitle: From the baroque capital to the Slavonian plains — a journey through Croatia's hidden side of vineyards, wetlands and fortified towns.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 18-25°C. Avoid July-August (heavy heat up to 35°C, frequent thunderstorms) and winter (cold, snow in Slavonian plains, fog in Zagreb).

title: North Dalmatia, Croatia Road Trip: Zadar, Krka National Park and Šibenik
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 22-28°C, pleasant sea, fewer crowds. Avoid July-August (intense heat up to 35°C, overcrowded beaches, high prices). Mild but rainy winters, occasional Bora wind on the coast.

title: Istria's Flavors & History: Roman Amphitheaters, Hill Towns & Truffle Forests
climate: Best period: May-June and September-October, temperatures 20-28°C, pleasant sea. Avoid July-August (intense heat up to 35°C, massive tourist crowds). Mild but rainy winter on the coast, cooler inland.

title: Secret Istria: Roman Ruins, Hilltop Villages and Truffle Forests
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, 20-25°C, ideal for hilltop villages and autumn truffle hunting. Avoid July-August (intense coastal heat, heavy tourist crowds). Mild but rainy winters in the inland hills.

title: Kvarner Bay Island Hopping: 10-Day Itinerary from Opatija to Rab, Croatia
climate: Best period: May-June and September-October, temperatures 20-28°C, sea 20-24°C, fewer crowds. Avoid July-August (intense heat 30-35°C, massive crowds on islands). Mild winter but windy, frequent bora winds on the coast.

title: Croatia National Parks Itinerary: Plitvice Lakes to Lastovo Island
climate: Best period: May-June and September-October, temperatures 20-28°C on the coast, 15-25°C at Plitvice. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 30-35°C, massive crowds) and December-February (Plitvice snowy, cold sea). Prefer May for Plitvice waterfalls at peak flow and September for warm sea (22-24°C) with fewer tourists.

title: Croatia's Heartland Road Trip: Zagreb to Slavonia's Hidden Villages
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 18-26°C, ideal for exploring the Slavonian plains. Avoid July-August (heavy heat regularly exceeding 30°C, frequent thunderstorms). Winter is cold and grey, not ideal for road trips.

title: South Dalmatia Road Trip: Split, Hvar and Dubrovnik Itinerary
climate: Best period: May-June and September-October, temperatures 22-28°C, pleasant sea and fewer crowds. Avoid July-August (intense heat up to 35°C, massive crowds, high prices). Mild winter but windy (bora) with frequent rain.

title: South Dalmatia Coastal Drive: Split to Dubrovnik via Makarska
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October (22-28°C), pleasant sea and moderate crowds. Avoid July-August: scorching heat (30-35°C), massive crowds in Dubrovnik, high prices. Winter is mild but many services close, and the bura wind can disrupt ferries.

title: What to See in Budapest: Architecture and Heritage
subtitle: Jewish memory, baroque palaces, Art Nouveau thermal baths and royal panoramas on the Danube — Budapest in 19 stops.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for walking tours. Avoid July-August (intense heat, 30-35°C) and December-February (frequent frost, -5 to 5°C). The Danube can be windy even in summer.

title: Hungary Road Trip: Thermal Baths, Lake Balaton and Budapest
subtitle: From the Austrian border to the vineyards of Tokaj — a loop through Hungary's thermal springs, medieval castles and baroque splendour.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for visiting. Avoid July-August (intense heat 30-35°C, frequent thunderstorms) and harsh continental winter (-5 to 5°C). Thermal baths accessible year-round.

title: Hungary Grand Tour: Thermal Baths, Lake Balaton and UNESCO Villages
subtitle: From Budapest to Pécs via Eger, Lillafüred and Lake Balaton — 7 days through baroque, Ottoman and thermal Hungary.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for thermal baths and cultural visits. Avoid July-August (intense heat up to 35°C, Lake Balaton overcrowded). Winter is harsh with frequent frost, though thermal baths remain appealing.

title: Around Budapest: A Cultural and Wellness Loop
subtitle: From the Danube Bend to the cellars of Tokaj, a grand circuit through the Hungarian heartland.
climate: Best period: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for exploring cities and vineyards. Avoid July-August (intense heat in Budapest, often 30-35°C with high humidity). Cold winters (-2 to 5°C) but thermal baths remain an option.

title: Alföld: Puszta, Thermal Baths, and the Great Plain in 9 Days
subtitle: From Budapest to Debrecen, a 9-day road trip through the heart of Hungary across steppes, thermal baths, and Art Nouveau treasures.
climate: Best period: May-June and September, temperatures 18-26°C, ideal for the puszta and sightseeing. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 30-35°C, little shade on the plain) and December-February (frequent frost, -5 to 5°C). Thermal baths open year-round.

title: Indonesia: Bali & Lombok — Ubud, Gili Islands and Kuta's Wild Coast
subtitle: From Ubud's jungle temples to Uluwatu's sea cliffs, through Sidemen's rice terraces, the crystal waters of Gili Air and the untouched beaches of southern Lombok.
climate: Best time: May to September, dry season with 27-32°C, ideal for beaches and diving in the Gilis. Avoid December-February (monsoon, heavy daily rains, rough seas making crossings to Lombok challenging). High humidity year-round.

title: Bali in 10 Days: From the Trendy South to the Hidden Rice Terraces of the East
subtitle: Seminyak beaches, Uluwatu cliffs, Ubud temples, Munduk crater lakes and Sidemen rice terraces — the complete tour of the Island of the Gods
climate: Best period: May to September, dry season with 27-30°C on the coast, 22-26°C in the highlands (Ubud, Munduk). Avoid December-March: intense monsoon, daily torrential rains, slippery mountain roads. High humidity year-round.

title: Java and Bali: Temples, Volcanoes and Rice Terraces in 14 Days
subtitle: From Yogyakarta to Uluwatu — a crossing of Indonesia's two most iconic islands, with UNESCO temples, active volcanoes and terraced rice paddies.
climate: Best time: May to September (dry season), 27-32°C in lowlands, 15-25°C at altitude (Bromo). Avoid December-February: heavy tropical rains, muddy temple paths, slippery mountain roads. July-August sees peak crowds at major sites.

title: Borneo–Java–Bali Expedition: Orangutans, Temples and Volcanoes
subtitle: 15 days from Kalimantan to Bali — from the tropical rainforests of Borneo to the Hindu temples of Java and the sacred cliffs of the Island of the Gods
climate: Best period: April to October (dry season), 27-32°C, high but bearable humidity. Avoid November-March: monsoon with daily torrential rains, roads to Bromo sometimes impassable. At Mount Bromo, bring warm clothes for sunrise (5-10°C).

title: Kalimantan: Orangutans, Primary Rainforests and the Derawan Islands in 15 Days
subtitle: From Balikpapan to Putussibau — a complete crossing of Indonesian Borneo, with orangutan sanctuaries, diving in the Derawan archipelago, the Banjarmasin floating market and Dayak longhouses.
climate: Best time: June to September, dry season with 26-32°C and less rain for jungle trekking and island access. Avoid November-March (daily heavy downpours, muddy forest roads impassable). Year-round high humidity across Kalimantan.

title: Northern Sumatra: Orangutans, Lake Toba and Tsunami Memorials in 14 Days
subtitle: From Medan to the Karo volcanoes, from Lake Toba to the rice paddies of Harau, all the way to the memorials of Banda Aceh — 14 days in the heart of Sumatra's most authentic landscapes
climate: Best period: June-September (dry season), temperatures 25-32°C in lowlands, 18-25°C at altitude (Berastagi, Lake Toba). Avoid October-December: daily torrential rains on the west coast (Padang, Bukittinggi), challenging mountain roads. High humidity year-round, bring mosquito repellent.

title: Sulawesi: Bunaken Reefs, Toraja Highlands and Wakatobi Lagoons in 20 Days
subtitle: From Bunaken's marine park to the funeral cliffs of Tana Toraja, through highland volcanoes and Wakatobi's record-breaking reefs — the grand tour of Indonesia's K-shaped island
climate: Best period: April to October (dry season), temperatures 27-32°C, ideal for diving in Bunaken and Wakatobi. Avoid November-March: heavy daily rainfall, rough seas, difficult mountain roads to Toraja. High humidity year-round.

title: The Grand Sumatra Tour: Orangutans, Lake Toba, Mentawai Shamans and Pagaralam Megaliths
subtitle: From the wild orangutans of Gunung Leuser to the tattooed shamans of Siberut, through the volcanic Lake Toba and the mysterious megaliths of Pagaralam — sixteen days across the untouched heart of Sumatra.
climate: Best period: May-September (dry season), temperatures 25-32°C in lowlands, 18-25°C at altitude (Lake Toba, Bukittinggi). Avoid October-March: heavy monsoon on west coast, daily rain, difficult access to Mentawai/Siberut due to rough seas. High humidity year-round.

title: Sumba: Megaliths, Ikat Weaving and Wild Beaches in 10 Days
subtitle: From Tambolaka to Waingapu, a crossing of the island of megalithic tombs — Marapu villages, ikat weaving, southern cliffs and lagoons, and the golden savannas of the east.
climate: Best period: May-October (dry season), temperatures 25-32°C with little rain. Avoid November-March: heavy rainfall makes dirt roads difficult to access. Sumba is drier than the rest of Indonesia, bring water during dry season.

title: West & Central Java: From Jakarta to the Dieng Temples and Solo's Royal Courts in 12 Days
subtitle: From colonial Batavia to the Javan rhinoceros of Ujung Kulon, from the Hindu temples of the Dieng Plateau to the batik workshops of Solo: 12 days across the forgotten half of Java.
climate: Best time: May-September, dry season with temperatures 26-32°C (cooler at altitude in Dieng and Wonosobo). Avoid December-February: daily torrential rains, difficult mountain roads. High humidity year-round.

title: Moluccas: Spices, Forts and Diving in the Banda Islands
subtitle: 11 days in the Spice Islands — from Ambon and Saparua to the Banda Islands where nutmeg was worth more than Manhattan, then on to the National Geographic sands of the Kei Islands
climate: Best period: October to April (dry season), temperatures 26-31°C, calm seas for diving. Avoid May-September: monsoon brings frequent rain and rough seas, limited inter-island boats. High humidity year-round.

title: Java, Bali, Lombok and Komodo: The Indonesian Island Odyssey in 17 Days
subtitle: From Borobudur to the jungles of Komodo, through the blue fire of Ijen, the rice terraces of Bali and the pristine beaches of Lombok — 17 days island-hopping across the Indonesian archipelago.
climate: Best period: May to October (dry season), temperatures 27-32°C with moderate humidity. Avoid December-February: heavy monsoon rains, rough seas making crossings to Komodo difficult or impossible. July-August: peak tourist crowds in Bali.

title: Flores West to East: Komodo, Wae Rebo, Ngada Villages and the Kelimutu Lakes
subtitle: A complete 14-day crossing of Flores — from Komodo's dragons to Ruteng's spider-web rice fields, from the conical houses of Wae Rebo to the megalithic altars of Bena, all the way to the three coloured lakes of Kelimutu volcano.
keywords: wae rebo traditional village mbaru niang
spider web rice fields ruteng lingko
flores indonesia off the beaten track
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 26-31°C, dry season perfect for Komodo boat trips. Avoid November-March (monsoon rains, rough seas, muddy roads to Wae Rebo). July-August very dry but strong winds at sea.

title: Raja Ampat in 12 Days: Reefs, Papuan Villages and the Lagoons of Wayag
subtitle: From the coral walls of Cape Kri — world record for biodiversity with 374 fish species on a single dive — to the turquoise karst lagoons of Wayag, the manta rays of Misool and the stilt villages of the Dampier Strait: 12 days through the underwater epicenter of the planet.
climate: Best period: October to April, dry season with calm seas, ideal for diving and island hopping (28-30°C). Avoid May-September: southeast monsoon brings daily rain, rough seas make boat transfers challenging. High humidity year-round.

title: Ireland Food Trail: Farmers Markets and Artisan Distilleries
subtitle: From Dublin to the Atlantic coast, a 11 days journey through the farmers markets, craft distilleries and producers who define Irish gastronomy.
climate: Best period: May-June and September, temperatures 15-18°C, moderate rainfall. Avoid October-March (continuous rain on the west coast, strong winds). Summer remains wet and July-August are crowded.

title: Ireland Road Trip: Capitals, Coasts and Mountains
subtitle: 16 days from Dublin to Belfast and back, through cliffs, pubs and monasteries lost in the mist
climate: Best time: May-June and September, temperatures 12-18°C, less rain than summer. Avoid October-March (continuous rain, strong winds on the west coast). July-August remains cool (15-20°C) with frequent showers and peak tourist crowds.

title: Secret Ireland Road Trip: Hidden Villages & Wild Coasts
subtitle: From Dalkey to Dublin, 11 days through Ireland's hidden villages—fishing ports, forgotten abbeys, secret cliffs and the Burren's moonscapes.
climate: Best period: May-June and September, temperatures 15-20°C, long days, moderate rainfall. Avoid November-February (cold, persistent rain, short days). West coast (Achill, Burren) faces strong Atlantic winds in winter.

title: The Ireland They Don't Show You — 10 Days
subtitle: From Dublin to Shannon in 10 days, through Neolithic tombs, the prison of the Rising, and an Atlantic monastery.
climate: Best time: May-June and September, temperatures 12-18°C, less rainfall. Avoid November-March (persistent rain, strong coastal winds, short days). Weather unpredictability requires layered clothing and waterproofs year-round.

title: Israel and Sinai Highlights: Sacred Sites and Timeless Desert Landscapes
climate: Best time: March-May and October-November, with temperatures of 20-28°C on the coast and in Jerusalem, 25-35°C in the desert. Avoid June-September: extreme heat in the Negev, Dead Sea, and Sinai (exceeding 40°C). Winter is mild during the day but nights are freezing in Saint Catherine.

title: India Grand Tour: From the Taj Mahal to the Backwaters
subtitle: 30 days across India by train, from the Mughal north to the Dravidian south, from Rajasthan's forts to Kerala's backwaters
climate: Best time: November to February, temperatures 20-30°C depending on region, dry season ideal for the entire route. Avoid April-June (scorching heat 40-45°C in the north) and June-September (intense monsoon, torrential rains across the country). Southern regions may experience rains in October-November (northeast monsoon).

title: Grand Northern India Circuit: Delhi, Varanasi & the Himalayas
subtitle: From the frenzy of Delhi to Himalayan peaks, from the ghats of Varanasi to the erotic temples of Khajuraho - 19 days through India at its most intense
climate: Best period: October to March, temperatures 15-25°C in the plains, cool in the mountains. Avoid April-June (extreme heat 40-45°C) and July-September (heavy monsoon in the east). Himalayan passes closed December-April.

title: Kerala Backwaters, Tea Hills & Coast Circuit
subtitle: From Kochi to Varkala in 12 days—a journey through coconut-fringed canals, mist-wrapped tea plantations, and cliffs plunging into the Arabian Sea.
climate: Best period: December-March, dry season with 23-32°C, ideal for backwaters and coast. Avoid June-September (intense monsoon, daily torrential rains) and April-May (stifling heat 35-38°C, extreme humidity). Munnar stays cooler at altitude year-round.

title: South India Circuit: Temples, Backwaters & Wellness
subtitle: From Kerala to Tamil Nadu, a journey through peaceful canals, tea-covered hills, and ancient Dravidian temples
climate: Best time: November to February, temperatures 22-30°C, low humidity. Avoid June-September (heavy monsoon in Kerala, torrential rains) and March-May (scorching heat 35-40°C, high humidity on the coast). Munnar cooler (15-25°C) at altitude.

title: Mumbai & Maharashtra Circuit: Sacred Caves and the Konkan Coast
subtitle: From Mumbai's frenzy to the ancient caves of Ajanta and Ellora in 12 days—a journey through colonial architecture, rock-cut temples, and unexpected vineyards
climate: Best time: November to February, temperatures 15-28°C, dry weather ideal for exploring caves and monuments. Avoid June to September (intense monsoon, torrential rains, difficult roads) and April-May (scorching heat 35-40°C, oppressive humidity). Konkan coast remains very humid year-round.

title: Intensive Rajasthan Road Trip: Forts, Tigers & Taj Mahal
subtitle: From Lake Pichola to the Thar dunes, 13 days of palaces, jungle and desert between Udaipur and Delhi
climate: Best time: November to February, temperatures 15-25°C, clear skies. Avoid April-June (extreme heat 40-48°C, sightseeing challenging). Light monsoon July-September but high humidity.

title: Rajasthan Road Trip: Desert Palaces and Sacred Lakes
subtitle: 9 days from Agra to Udaipur, through Mughal marble, pink and blue cities, sacred lakes, and desert fortresses
climate: Best time: October to March, temperatures 15-28°C, ideal for exploring forts and palaces. Avoid April-June (scorching heat 40-48°C, desert unbearable). July-September: moderate monsoon but high humidity.

title: South India Circuit: Hampi, Goa and the Nilgiri Hills
subtitle: From the majestic ruins of Vijayanagara to the hidden beaches of southern Goa, via the colonial canals of Kochi and the tea plantations of the Nilgiris
climate: Best time: November to February, temperatures 22-30°C, dry and sunny. Avoid the monsoon (June-September) on the west coast, and April-May (scorching heat, 38-42°C). The Nilgiris stay cool year-round (15-25°C).

title: Tamil Nadu and Kerala Road Trip: Temples and Backwaters
subtitle: 12 days among carved gopurams, merchant palaces, spice forests, and coconut-fringed lagoons
climate: Best period: November to March, temperatures 25-32°C, ideal for temples and backwaters. Avoid monsoon season (June-September, torrential rains in Kerala) and April-May (scorching heat up to 40°C). High humidity year-round, especially along the coast.

title: Deccan Trail: Bahmani Sultans and Kakatiya Temples in 12 Days
subtitle: A 12-day circuit linking Hyderabad's Bahmani fortresses, Kakatiya temples, and the banks of the Krishna River.
climate: Best time: November-February, temperatures 15-28°C, dry and sunny. Avoid March-May (extreme heat, 38-45°C) and June-September (intense monsoon, difficult roads). October remains humid, stick to winter.

title: India's East Coast: Chennai to Visakhapatnam, 13 Days
subtitle: This coastal journey traces India's eastern shore from Chennai's Dravidian temples to the beaches of Visakhapatnam.
climate: Best period: January-March, temperatures 25-32°C, calm sea. Avoid April-May (scorching heat 38-42°C, oppressive humidity) and October-December (northeast monsoon, heavy rains along the entire coast, cyclone risk). July-September: lighter monsoon but rain possible.

title: Gujarat: Grand UNESCO Sites Tour in 15 Days
subtitle: This circuit links the historic city of Ahmedabad to the white desert of Kutch in 15 days, through Gujarat's greatest treasures.
climate: Best time: November to February, mild temperatures 15-28°C, clear skies. Avoid April-June (scorching heat 40-45°C, Kutch roads challenging) and July-September (monsoon season). The Rann of Kutch desert is spectacular in winter but unbearable the rest of the year.

title: From Coast to Jungle: Bhubaneswar to Bhopal in 16 Days
subtitle: A 16-day road trip through solar temples, tiger reserves, and prehistoric caves.
climate: Best time: November to February, 20-30°C, clear skies. Absolutely avoid April-June (extreme heat 38-45°C) and July-September (heavy monsoon on the coast, difficult roads). Cyclone risk in October-November along Odisha.

title: Wild Iceland: Snæfellsnes & Westfjords in 8 Days
subtitle: Iceland off the beaten path, from Snæfellsnes volcanoes to Europe's most remote fjords
climate: Best period: June to August, temperatures 10-15°C, nearly continuous daylight. Avoid September-May: Westfjords roads often closed, severe storms, partial polar night in winter. Always pack warm, waterproof layers—conditions change within hours.

title: Iceland Road Trip: The Ring Road in 12 Days
subtitle: Complete circuit around the island via the Ring Road, from Reykjavik to the eastern fjords through the southern glaciers and northern volcanoes
climate: Best period: June to August, temperatures 10-15°C, endless daylight and Ring Road fully accessible. Avoid November to March (near-permanent darkness, severe storms, road closures). Wind and rain possible year-round, weather changes within hours.

title: Complete Iceland: Highlands, Glaciers & Fjords in 18 Days
subtitle: The ultimate Icelandic adventure combining the Ring Road with remote Highlands - Landmannalaugar, Kerlingarfjöll, Vatnajökull glaciers and eastern fjords
climate: Mandatory period: mid-June to early September (10-15°C), the only window when Highland F-roads are open. Rest of the year, the central highlands and Landmannalaugar are completely inaccessible. Pack warm and waterproof layers even in summer: wind, rain, and abrupt weather changes occur anytime.

title: Iceland in 12 Days: Ring Road of Elves, Trolls & Volcanoes
subtitle: A 12-day loop follows Route 1 from Reykjavík to Snæfellsnes, crossing geysers, glaciers, black beaches and the land of Icelandic folklore.
climate: Best period: June-August, temperatures 10-15°C and very long days (midnight sun). Avoid November-March: closed roads, frequent storms and darkness. Constant wind year-round, weather changes within hours.

title: Italy's Most Beautiful Villages in Abruzzo
subtitle: From the Adriatic coast to the peaks of Gran Sasso and Majella, a tour of Abruzzo's most beautiful medieval villages in 12 nights
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, with temperatures of 15-25°C in the lowlands and 10-20°C at altitude. Avoid July-August (scorching heat in Vasto, up to 35°C) and winter in mountain villages (snow, roads sometimes impassable above 1000m). Spring offers lush green landscapes in the Apennines.

title: Basilicata: From Matera to Pollino, Castles, Ghost Towns, and the Lucanian Dolomites
subtitle: A 9-day road trip through Basilicata, from the Norman castles of Melfi to the Sassi of Matera, via the Lucanian Dolomites, the ghost village of Craco, the clay badlands of Aliano, the Maratea coast, and the ancient forests of Pollino.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 16-26°C. Avoid July-August: scorching heat in Matera's sassi (35-40°C), uncomfortable hiking in the Lucanian Dolomites. Mild winters in lowlands, cold and snowy on Pollino massif.

title: 10 Natural Wonders Around Bologna
subtitle: From Po plain wetlands to Apennine summits, a nature and village circuit through Bologna's backcountry
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 20-28°C in the plains, cooler at altitude. Avoid July-August (oppressive heat, 32-35°C, high humidity) and winter (persistent fog in lowlands, snow in the Apennines). Mountain sites require passable roads outside winter season.

title: Calabria: From Cosenza to Reggio, Between Mountains and Ionian Sea
subtitle: A 6 days road trip through wild Calabria, from the perched villages of Pollino to the white cliffs of Tropea and the ancient bronzes of Reggio.
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, temperatures 20-26°C, pleasant sea. Avoid July-August: frequent heatwaves (35-40°C), especially inland; Sila mountains stay cooler. Mild coastal winter (12-15°C) but rainy.

title: Complete Calabria Loop: Tyrrhenian and Ionian Coasts
subtitle: From Pizzo and Tropea on the Tyrrhenian coast to the Riace Bronzes in Reggio, then up the Ionian shore via Gerace, Crotone, and the ruins of Magna Graecia

title: From Naples to Calabria: The Tyrrhenian Coast
subtitle: From Paestum's Greek temples to Maratea's wild coast, the essential stops between Naples and Calabria along the Tyrrhenian Sea

title: Hidden Villages of the Amalfi Coast: From Praiano to Salerno
subtitle: From Praiano's fjord to Salerno and Europe's first medical school, discover the lesser-known villages of the Amalfi Coast between cliffs and IGP anchovies
climate: Best period: April-June and September-October, temperatures 20-26°C, pleasant sea. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 30-35°C, massive crowds, sky-high prices) and November-March (frequent rain, coastal road sometimes closed). Summer heat is intensified by maritime humidity.

title: From Naples to the Lucanian Dolomites: Secret Campania and the Amalfi Coast
subtitle: An 9 days journey through Naples' alleyways, Procida's pastel houses, Caserta's Italian Versailles, Irpinia's forgotten villages, Castelmezzano's dizzying cliffs, and the panoramic Amalfi Coast.

climate: Best period: June to September, daytime temperatures 15-25°C, cool evenings. Avoid November-April (heavy snow, mountain passes closed). Afternoon thunderstorms common in summer at higher elevations.

subtitle: A 7 days road trip along Emilia-Romagna's eastern coast, through Renaissance palaces, flamingo-filled lagoons, Byzantine mosaics and Roman ruins.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 18-26°C, ideal for Ravenna's mosaics and the Po Delta. Avoid July-August (muggy heat 30-35°C, Rimini beaches packed). Mild coastal winter but grey and windy.

climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 18-27°C. Avoid July-August (scorching heat up to 35°C, high humidity). Cold winter with frequent fog in the Po Valley.

subtitle: From Rimini's Roman ruins to the Republic of San Marino, a journey back through time among Malatesta fortresses, Villanovan necropolises, and Montefeltro citadels
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, temperatures 18-25°C. Avoid July-August (heavy heat up to 32°C, high humidity on the coast). The hinterland stays a few degrees cooler.

Avoid July-August (heavy heat up to 32°C in lowlands) and December-February (cold, persistent fog, frequent frost near Venzone). Spring thunderstorms possible in mountain areas.

subtitle: From Milan's contemporary galleries to Siena's Piazza del Campo, twelve days through Lombard design, UNESCO cities, Emilian gastronomy, and Tuscan Renaissance

subtitle: From the mill town of Crespi d'Adda to lakeside fortresses, a journey through ten centuries of Lombard history

subtitle: From Bracciano's Renaissance castle to the volcanic lakes of the Castelli Romani, 3 days through hilltop villages, noble villas, and ancient sanctuaries in the Roman countryside

Avoid July-August (scorching heat 30-35°C, little shade in villages) and January-February (cold and damp, possible snow at altitude). The hilly terrain brings cooler air in summer but makes some villages harder to reach in winter.

subtitle: From Ninfa's medieval Pompeii to Ponza's Roman caves
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 18-25°C, ideal for caves and gardens. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 30-35°C, crowded beaches). Mild winter but rainy; rough seas to Ponza.

subtitle: From the Lombard metropolis to Ligurian harbors, from Pisa's leaning towers to Florence's frescoes, down to Rome's imperial ruins—15 days along Italy's classical spine.

subtitle: Alpine lakes, medieval towns, and soaring Dolomite peaks in 15 days
climate: Best time: May-June and September-early October, temperatures 20-26°C, ideal for lakes and mountain hiking. subtitle: Twenty days through Italy's greatest treasures—from Alpine valleys to medieval towers, Renaissance Florence to ancient Rome, and finally the dramatic cliffs and azure waters of the Tyrrhenian coast.

climate: Best period: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for villas and lakes. Avoid July-August (oppressive heat up to 35°C, massive crowds in Venice and Lake Garda). Cold winter with frequent fog across the Po plain.

title: Emilia-Romagna's Hidden Treasures: Castles, Apennines & the Great Labyrinth
climate: Best period: May-June and September-October, temperatures 18-26°C, ideal for castles and mountain trails. Avoid July-August (oppressive heat 30-35°C, heavy humidity in the plains) and December-February (cold, thick fog in the Po Valley, snow in mountains). Watch for frequent fog in autumn-winter at lower elevations.

climate: Best period: April-May and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for visiting Langhe vineyards. title: Hidden Beaches in Italy: Secluded Coves and Turquoise Waters
climate: Best time: May-June and September-mid-October, temperatures 22-28°C, pleasant sea, uncrowded beaches. Avoid July-August (scorching heat in the south, crowds even at hidden coves, prices doubled). Mild winter but many services closed, cool sea (14-16°C).

subtitle: The complete loop from Bari: wild Gargano, baroque Lecce, UNESCO trulli, and Matera's ancient sassi in 19 days.

subtitle: 4 days to traverse twenty centuries of history, from Piazza di Spagna to the Vatican domes, passing through ancient arenas, Trastevere's alleys, and Caravaggio's masterpieces.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for walking the historic center. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 30-35°C, massive tourist crowds). Mild winter (8-15°C) but rainy.

subtitle: From Cagliari to UNESCO nuraghi, from Alghero's cliffs to Orgosolo's murals—a full circuit through Sardinia's many faces.

subtitle: From Palermo to the Greek temples of Agrigento, from the baroque towns of Ragusa to Mount Etna and Syracuse—the grand island tour in fourteen days.

climate: Best period: April-May and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for exploring Tuscan hills. Avoid July-August: oppressive heat (30-35°C), heavy tourist crowds. Winter (December-February) is cool and damp, less suitable for touring.

title: The Most Beautiful Villages of Grosseto Province
subtitle: From medieval mining towns to Etruscan tufa cliffs, from Spanish coastal forts to granite islands: ten stops through the deep Maremma.

title: Casentino Circuit: Art, Villages and Sanctuaries
subtitle: From the lakeside villages of Umbria to the sacred forests of the Casentino, through the frescoes of Piero della Francesca and the medieval hilltop towns of the Valtiberina
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-26°C, ideal for exploring villages and sanctuaries. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 30-35°C in lowlands, heavy tourist crowds). The Casentino's altitude brings cooler air even in summer—pack a sweater for La Verna and Camaldoli.

title: Lucca Province Circuit: Hill Towns and Coast

title: From Lunigiana to Cinque Terre: Between Tuscany and Liguria

title: Tuscany & Cinque Terre Road Trip: Renaissance Art, Chianti Hills & Coastal Villages
subtitle: From Pisa to the towers of San Gimignano, from Chianti vineyards to the suspended villages of the Cinque Terre—a journey through medieval Tuscany, the Etruscan coast, and the Ligurian Riviera.

title: Umbria Itinerary: Medieval Villages & Rolling Hills
subtitle: From the tuff city of Orvieto to the shores of Lake Trasimeno, ten stages through Umbria's most unspoiled medieval hamlets.

Castelluccio di Norcia offers spectacular blooms late May-early July but stays cool at altitude. Winter cold and damp (5-12°C), not recommended.

title: Aosta Valley Circuit: From Roman Gateway to Mont Blanc

title: Vicenza Circuit: Palladian Villas and the Venetian Countryside

title: Gargano & Northern Puglia - The Spur of the Italian Boot

climate: Best period: May-June and September-October, temperatures 22-28°C, pleasant sea conditions. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 35°C+, overcrowded). Mild winter but windy, frequent rain from November to March.

title: Calabria - From Tropea to the Giants of Sila

title: Authentic Molise in 3 Days: A Loop Through the Heart of Samnium

title: Grand Tour of Molise in 9 Days: Pescara to Naples Through the Samnium
subtitle: A point-to-point journey from Pescara to Naples that cuts deep through Italy's least-visited region: Adriatic coast, Samnite and Roman archaeology, Mainarde mountains, millennial crafts and perched villages.

title: Marche & Abruzzo in 8 Days: From Rimini to Pescara, Hills to Wild Nature

title: Puglia: 3 days through the Murgia Barese, from Polignano's cliffs to the Tarantine ravines
subtitle: A 3 days loop descending from Bari to Taranto via whitewashed villages, limestone gorges and traditional pottery workshops.

subtitle: An 3 days loop from Brindisi through fortified villages, Greek-speaking towns of Salento, Renaissance castles and marine caves, all the way to Otranto's cathedral.

title: Piedmont - 3 days in Nebbiolo country, from Torino to Barolo
subtitle: A 3 days itinerary explores the UNESCO-listed Langhe and Monferrato, from Torino vermouth to Serralunga Barolo, via Alba white truffles and Canelli's underground cathedrals.

title: Latvia Road Trip: Medieval Castles, Baltic Coast and Soviet-Era History

title: Soviet Legacy and Wine: Moldova's Capital and Countryside Treasures

title: Montenegro Adventure: Bay of Kotor, Durmitor National Park and Ancient Towns

title: Classic Madagascar Grand Tour: RN7, Baobabs, Pangalanes & Sainte-Marie Island
