title: Grand Mongolia Tour: Altai, Gobi, and Central Steppe
subtitle: 20 days from end to end - from the Kazakh Altai to the Gobi dunes, through volcanic lakes and Genghis Khan's ancient capital
climate: Best time: late May-June and September, daytime temperatures 15-25°C, cool nights. Avoid July-August (frequent storms, scorching Gobi at 40°C+) and April-May (strong winds, sandstorms). Extreme winter (-30°C) unsuitable for this route.

title: Mongolia Road Trip: Gobi Desert, Orkhon Valley & Lake Khövsgöl
subtitle: 19 days across desert, central steppe and the great northern lake — entirely by 4x4, no internal flights.
climate: Best time: June to September, daytime temperatures 15-25°C. Avoid November-March entirely (temperatures -20 to -40°C, roads impassable). The Gobi Desert experiences extreme day-night temperature swings; Lake Khövsgöl stays frozen until May.

title: Martinique Circuit: Mount Pelée and Beaches
subtitle: From Saint-Pierre's volcanic ruins to white-sand southern beaches, via Mount Pelée and historic Creole estates.
climate: Best time: December to April (dry season), temperatures 26-30°C, calm seas. Avoid August-October (high hurricane risk) and June-November (daily rain, heavy humidity). Mount Pelée often shrouded in morning clouds.

title: Mauritius Circuit: The Essentials in 10 Days
subtitle: From resort beaches in the north to wild southern coasts - lagoons, forests, cliffs, and Creole heritage in a clockwise loop.
climate: Best period: May to October (austral winter), temperatures 20-25°C, dry and sunny. Avoid January-March: cyclone risk, heavy rainfall, and humid heat (30-33°C). East coast experiences stronger trade winds year-round.

title: Mauritius in Depth: 15 Days from the Interior to the Coast
subtitle: Central plateau, Tea Route, endemic forests, wild south, and beach rewards – the complete journey to truly understand Mauritius.
climate: Best time: May to November, temperatures 20-26°C, dry and sunny. Avoid January-March (cyclone risk, heavy rainfall, humid heat up to 33°C). The central plateau (Curepipe) stays cooler year-round.

title: Family Mauritius - 15 days around the island, from the southeast to Le Morne
subtitle: Fifteen days of excursions from four coastal bases to swim with dolphins, spot whales, try buggies and explore the island with children.
climate: Best time: May to December, temperatures 20-28°C, calm seas and little rain. Avoid January to March (cyclones, humid heat up to 33°C, heavy downpours). East coast receives more rain than west year-round.

title: Baja California Road Trip Mexico: From Desert to the Sea of Cortez
subtitle: 14-day one-way journey from Tijuana to Los Cabos: desert landscapes, whales, wine country, missions, and coral reefs.
climate: Best time: November to April, temperatures 20-28°C, clear skies and cool desert nights. Avoid July-September (extreme desert heat 40°C+, Pacific hurricane risk). Very arid region: bring water and sun protection year-round.

title: Wild Northern Mexico: Huasteca, Zacatecas & Copper Canyon
subtitle: 14 days of adventure from San Luis Potosí to Chihuahua: tropical jungle, ghost town, colonial cities, and the legendary train along canyon edges.
keywords: el chepe train barrancas del cobre
climate: Best time: October to April, dry season with 15-28°C depending on altitude (canyon, plateau, coast). Avoid June-September: torrential rains in the Huasteca and Copper Canyon, roads sometimes impassable. In winter, bring warm clothes for the canyon heights (freezing possible).

title: Mexico Road Trip: From the Mayan Caribbean to Mexico City
subtitle: The grand 21-day tour of Mexico, from Cancún to Mexico City. This is the classic route—the one we recommend for a first visit. Start with the beaches and cenotes of the Yucatán, step back in time at Chichén Itzá and Uxmal, plunge into the jungle at Palenque, climb to new altitudes in San Cristóbal, get lost in the markets of Oaxaca, and finish in the Aztec megacity. The Cancún → CDMX direction makes sense: save the cultural knockout of Mexico City for the end, when you're warmed up. One logistical note: the San Cristóbal → Oaxaca leg (Day 14) takes 10-11 hours by road—we recommend the domestic flight (1 hour) to keep your energy up.
climate: Best time: November to March, with 22-28°C on the coast and 15-25°C at altitude (Mexico City, San Cristóbal). Avoid May-June (scorching heat in Yucatan, up to 40°C) and September-October (heavy rains, hurricane risk on the coast). Pack warm layers for cool evenings at altitude in San Cristóbal (2100m) and Mexico City.

title: Mexico Pacific Coast & Colonial Cities: Mexico City to Puerto Vallarta
subtitle: 14 days exploring the capital, colonial towns, Michoacán, Guadalajara, and Pacific beaches
climate: Best time: November to April, dry season with 20-28°C on the coast and 15-25°C in highlands (Mexico City, colonial towns). Avoid July-September: daily torrential rains, oppressive humidity in Puerto Vallarta (30-35°C). Cool nights at altitude even in winter.

title: Mexico Road Trip: Oaxaca & Southern Pacific Coast
subtitle: From Zapotec markets and Monte Albán ruins to the wild Pacific beaches—a journey for those who want to truly know Oaxaca.
climate: Best period: November-April, temperatures 25-32°C on the Pacific coast, 15-25°C in Oaxaca and Mexico City. Avoid June-October: heavy daily rainfall, hurricane risk on the coast (Puerto Escondido, Huatulco), roads sometimes impassable. Mexico City and Oaxaca remain visitable year-round but expect afternoon downpours in summer.

title: Northeast Mexico: Monterrey, Desert & Mapimí, 12 Days
subtitle: From the Sierra Madre to the white dunes of Coahuila, a 12-day road trip through canyons, caves, and vineyards.
climate: Best period: March-May and October-November, temperatures 20-28°C. Avoid June-September (scorching heat 35-42°C in the desert, severe thunderstorms). Winter mild during the day but freezing nights in Mapimí desert (down to 0°C).

title: Borneo Explorer: 12 Days Through Sarawak & Sabah
subtitle: Kuching and its proboscis monkeys, Mulu's giant caves, the Kinabatangan River where pygmy elephants and orangutans cross paths—twelve days on the Malaysian side of Borneo.
climate: Best time: March-April and July-October, temperatures 26-32°C with moderate rainfall. Avoid November-February (northeast monsoon brings heavy rain to Sabah, difficult access to Mulu). Constant heat and humidity year-round (80-90%), pack light breathable clothing and rain gear.

title: Grand Malaysia Circuit: Peninsula & Borneo in 22 Days
subtitle: From KL's skyline to Cameron's misty tea terraces, George Town's murals to the Perhentians' reef sharks, then across the South China Sea to Borneo's proboscis monkeys, cave cathedrals, wild orangutans, and the last pygmy elephants on Earth.
climate: Best time: March-April and July-September, temperatures 25-32°C with less rain. Avoid November-February on the east coast (Perhentian): heavy monsoon, resorts closed. Equatorial humid climate year-round, frequent thunderstorms even in dry season.

title: Peninsular Malaysia Circuit: From KL to the Perhentian Islands
subtitle: Kuala Lumpur, the tea hills of Cameron Highlands, George Town's colonial lanes, and the Perhentian reefs—8 days between cities and beaches.
climate: Best period: March to October, temperatures 27-33°C in lowlands, 15-25°C in Cameron Highlands. Avoid November-February: northeast monsoon brings heavy rain, rough seas, and Perhentian Islands closures. Expect high humidity year-round with frequent afternoon thunderstorms.

title: Namibia 4x4 Self-Drive Safari: 16-Day Rooftop Tent Adventure
subtitle: From Sossusvlei dunes to Etosha safaris, the ultimate family adventure in the heart of the desert
climate: Best period: May to October (dry season), daytime temperatures 15-25°C, nights 0-10°C. Avoid December-March: extreme desert heat (40°C+), occasional rains, roads sometimes impassable. Pack warm layers for cold nights, even in summer.

title: Namibia-Botswana Safari: Sossusvlei, Etosha & Okavango Delta - 21 Days
subtitle: This itinerary was created by Mungo Park, a Geneva-based agency specializing in tailor-made African travel. If you'd like it customized to your needs, contact Mégane at +41 22 940 22 22 or via mungopark.ch/. Budget with agency assistance is around 450 euros per person per night. Please note our tool currently doesn't account for dirt tracks and may show detours that don't exist.
climate: Best time: May to October (dry season), daytime 20-28°C, cool nights (5-15°C). Avoid November-March: scorching heat (35-40°C), heavy rains, and increased malaria risk. Okavango floods peak June-August, perfect for wildlife viewing as animals gather at waterholes.

title: New Caledonia Journey: UNESCO Lagoon and Kanak Culture
subtitle: Ten days linking Nouméa to the edge of the world: UNESCO-listed lagoon, Hienghène's black limestone cliffs, Fort Teremba's colonial penal history, and traditional pirogue across Upi Bay on the Isle of Pines.
climate: Best period: May to October (dry season), temperatures 20-26°C, calm seas, ideal for lagoon visits. Avoid January-March: high cyclone risk, humid heat (28-32°C), heavy rainfall. November-December workable but requires weather monitoring.

title: Authentic Nicaragua: 24 days Itinerary Through Rivers, Lagoons and Caribbean Islands
subtitle: A Caribbean adventure through volcanoes, rainforests and paradise beaches
climate: Best time: December to April (dry season), temperatures 26-32°C. Avoid May to October: heavy rainfall on the Caribbean coast (Bluefields, Corn Islands), muddy roads to El Castillo, hurricane risk June-November. Pacific coast and lakes remain more accessible during rainy season.

title: Nicaragua Grand Loop: Volcanoes, Colonial Cities and Pacific Beaches
climate: Best time: November-March, temperatures 25-32°C on the Pacific coast, 20-28°C in the highlands (Esteli, Matagalpa). Avoid May-October: daily torrential rains and hurricane risk from September to November. April is very hot and dry.

title: Nicaragua's Caribbean & Islands: San Juan River, Corn Islands & Little Corn
climate: Best time: February to April, temperatures 28-32°C, dry season even on the Caribbean coast. Avoid September-October (torrential daily rains, difficult navigation, hurricane risk). The Caribbean coast and Corn Islands remain humid year-round—bring rain gear even in dry season.

title: Ultimate Netherlands Road Trip: Canals, Cheese & Colorful Blooms in 12 days
climate: Best time: mid-April to mid-May for tulips in bloom (Lisse, Keukenhof) and May-September for the rest, temperatures 15-23°C. Avoid November-March: cold, constant biting wind, frequent rain (2-8°C). Always bring windproof layers, even in summer.

title: Southern Netherlands Road Trip: Tulips, Windmills, Delta Works & Art Cities
subtitle: From The Hague to Haarlem via Maastricht and Keukenhof - 9 days exploring the best of southern Holland
climate: Best time: mid-March to mid-May for tulip fields in bloom, temperatures 10-18°C. Avoid November-February (frequent rain, cold winds, no flowers). The country stays windy year-round, bring a windbreaker even in spring.

title: Northern Netherlands Road Trip: Amsterdam, Friesland, Wadden Islands & Giethoorn
subtitle: From Golden Age canals to wild Wadden Sea islands and the car-free Venice of Overijssel
climate: Best time: May-June and September, temperatures 15-22°C, maximum sunshine. Avoid November-March (cold, wet, grey, icy winds on Texel). Rain possible year-round, bring a windbreaker.

title: Eastern Netherlands - Hanse, Veluwe and Achterhoek in 10 Days
subtitle: Ten days of Hanseatic towns, hidden castles and the best Van Gogh outside Amsterdam.
climate: Best time: May-September, temperatures 15-23°C, optimal sunshine for Giethoorn and Veluwe National Park. Avoid November-March (grey, cold and wet 2-8°C, frequent rain). Always pack a raincoat—showers possible even in summer.

title: Northern Netherlands: Wadden, Friesland & Groningen in 13 days
subtitle: From Amsterdam to the canals of Giethoorn in 13 days, via the Wadden Islands, Friesland, and Groningen.
climate: Best time: May to September, temperatures 18-22°C, peak sunshine on the Wadden Islands. Avoid November-March: biting coastal winds, frequent rain, reduced ferry schedules. Bring windproof layers even in summer—the Wadden coast is windy year-round.

title: Northern Norway Road Trip: Tromsø, Alta & North Cape
subtitle: From Tromsø's Arctic Cathedral to the 307m cliffs of North Cape, via the glaciers of the Lyngen Alps and the UNESCO rock carvings of Alta
climate: Best period: June-August, temperatures 10-18°C with midnight sun, ideal for the North Cape and fjords. Avoid November-March (polar night, snow-covered roads, -10 to -20°C). Pack warm clothes even in summer, strong winds common at the North Cape.

title: Central Norway Road Trip: Ålesund, Geirangerfjord, Trollstigen & Trondheim
subtitle: Art Nouveau architecture, UNESCO fjords, and vertiginous mountain roads: 11 days between Ålesund, Geiranger, and the world's northernmost Gothic cathedral.
climate: Best time: June-August, temperatures 12-20°C, Trollstigen road open. Avoid October-May: Trollstigen closed, constant rain and snow in the fjords. Even in summer, bring waterproof clothing (frequent rainfall on the coast).

title: Arctic Norway Road Trip: Tromsø, Senja & the Lofoten Islands
subtitle: From Tromsø's concrete Arctic Cathedral to the red fishing villages of the Lofoten, via Senja's Devil's Teeth and Bodø's roaring maelstrom.
climate: Best time: June to August, with midnight sun and temperatures of 10-18°C, but frequent rain even in summer. Avoid October-April (polar night, snow-covered roads, many mountain passes closed). Changeable Arctic weather: pack waterproof and warm clothing year-round.

title: Norway Road Trip by Train: Oslo, Fjords & Bergen
subtitle: From Oslo to UNESCO-listed fjords, from the Flåm Railway to Bergen: 12 days through Norway's most dramatic landscapes.
climate: Best time: May-September, temperatures 12-22°C, maximum daylight. Avoid October-March (constant rain in Bergen, limited fjord access, endless winter nights). Bergen gets 240 rainy days annually: pack waterproofs even in summer.

title: Southern Norway Road Trip: Stavanger, Preikestolen, Lysefjord & the South Coast
subtitle: From Stavanger's white wooden town to the cliffs of Lysefjord, then along the wild coast to Kristiansand
climate: Best period: June-August, temperatures 15-20°C, long days for hiking (Preikestolen, Kjerag). Avoid October-March: constant rain, muddy or closed trails, early darkness. The south coast gets less rain than Bergen but always pack waterproofs.

title: The Grand Tour - New Zealand in Its Entirety
subtitle: 27 days to see it all: Far North, East Cape, Wellington, West Coast, Queenstown, Milford Sound, Kaikoura and Akaroa - nothing left behind.
climate: Best time: November to March, temperatures 18-26°C, ideal for both islands. Avoid June-August: mountain roads closed (Milford, Mt Cook), frequent snow in the South, cold and rainy in the North. The West Coast stays very wet year-round (pack rain gear even in summer).

title: The Great Auckland-Christchurch Traverse: 22 Days Across New Zealand
subtitle: 22 days crossing both islands end to end, from Waiheke vineyards to Milford fjords, via West Coast glaciers and the wild Catlins coastline.
climate: Best time: October to April (Southern Hemisphere summer), temperatures 15-25°C depending on region. Avoid June-August: winter season, snow on mountain roads (Mount Cook, Queenstown), cold and wet weather. Pack waterproofs for the west coast (Franz Josef, Punakaiki): heavy rainfall year-round.

title: New Zealand: The Essential Two-Week Journey
subtitle: From Auckland to Queenstown, the classic two-island circuit - volcanoes, fjords, wild coastlines and Māori culture in 16 days.
climate: Best time: October-November and March-April, temperatures 12-22°C, fewer crowds and reasonable prices. Avoid June-August (cold in the mountains, roads sometimes closed to Mount Cook and Queenstown, snow). Changeable weather year-round: pack layers and waterproofs.

title: Oman Road Trip: Wadis, Desert and Mountains
subtitle: From the capital to the eastern coast, the Wahiba Desert and the heights of Jebel Akhdar: ten days to discover authentic Oman.
climate: Best time: November-March, temperatures 22-28°C, ideal for desert and wadis. Absolutely avoid May-September (extreme heat 40-48°C, oppressive coastal humidity, desert dangerous). Cool nights at Jebel Akhdar in winter (5-10°C), bring warm clothes.

title: Panama Circuit: Canal, Volcanoes & San Blas Islands
subtitle: From Panama City's engineering marvels to the pristine Guna Yala archipelago, via Chiriquí's volcanic highlands and the Caribbean beaches of Bocas del Toro.
climate: Best time: December to April (dry season), 25-32°C on coasts, 15-25°C in Boquete. Avoid September-November (heavy rains in Bocas and San Blas, rough seas). Bocas del Toro stays wet even in dry season, bring rain gear.

title: Off the Beaten Path Panama: Caribbean Coast, Azuero & Pacific Islands
subtitle: Atlantic canal passage, pirate fortresses of Portobelo, Azuero folklore, Santa Fé highlands and the Pearl Islands - authentic Panama beyond the tourist trail.
climate: Best time: January-April (dry season), temperatures 27-32°C, calm seas for island hopping. Avoid May-November: daily torrential rain, oppressive humidity, rough seas. Caribbean coast (Portobelo) stays humid even during dry season.

title: Essential Peru Journey: Lima to Machu Picchu
subtitle: From Lima to the Paracas cliffs, from Huacachina's dunes to Nazca's geoglyphs, from Arequipa's white streets to Cusco's Inca stones and Machu Picchu.
climate: Best time: May to September, dry season in the Andes with daytime temperatures of 18-22°C (cold nights at altitude). Avoid December-March: heavy rainfall in Cusco and Machu Picchu area, potential Inca Trail closures. The coast (Lima, Paracas) is accessible year-round but foggy June-September.

title: The Grand Southern Peru Circuit: Coast, Andes & Machu Picchu
subtitle: From Lima's coastal cliffs to the Ica dunes, from the depths of Colca Canyon to Lake Titicaca at 3,812m, all the way to Cusco and Machu Picchu—21 days through the heart of Andean civilization.
climate: Best period: May to September (dry season in the Andes), daytime temperatures 15-20°C in Cusco, cool nights, coast stable year-round. Avoid December-March: daily rains at altitude, closure risks (Machu Picchu, mountain roads), February worst. Plan for acclimatization in Cusco/Puno (3,400-3,800m), significant temperature swings.

title: Northern Peru Road Trip: Ancient Civilizations and Andes
subtitle: From Lima to the Andes and the cloud forest: 16 days tracing the footsteps of northern Peru's pre-Inca civilizations.
climate: Best time: May to September, dry season in the Andes (15-20°C daytime in Huaraz, 20-25°C on the coast). Avoid December-March: torrential rains in Chachapoyas and Huaraz, mountain roads become impassable. Bring warm clothing for high-altitude stops (Huaraz, Cajamarca).

title: Complete Polynesia: Society & Tuamotu Islands in 20 Days
subtitle: From Papeete to the atolls of Rangiroa and Fakarava, a journey between volcanoes, lagoons and shark passes across two archipelagos
keywords: tahiti moorea bora bora rangiroa fakarava
climate: Best time: May to October, dry season with 24-28°C and low humidity, ideal for diving and island hopping. Avoid November-March: humid heat (30-32°C), heavy rainfall, and tropical cyclone risk. Water temperature stays 26-29°C year-round.

title: Tahiti, Moorea & Bora Bora Circuit: Lagoons and Mountains
subtitle: From Papeete to Raiatea, a journey through turquoise lagoons, eroded volcanoes and sacred marae at the heart of the Polynesian triangle
climate: Best period: May to October (dry season), 24-28°C, optimal sunshine for lagoons. Avoid November-April: humid heat (up to 32°C), heavy rainfall and cyclone risk. Humidity remains high year-round.

title: Visayas Circuit: Cebu, Bohol and Siquijor in 14 Days
subtitle: From Cebu's colonial streets to Bohol's chocolate hills and Siquijor's turquoise waterfalls, a journey through the central Visayas
climate: Best time: December to May (dry season), temperatures 26-32°C with constant humid heat. Avoid June to November: southwest monsoon brings heavy rains and typhoon risk. The Visayas are less exposed than northern Philippines but remain vulnerable in September-October.

title: Palawan Circuit: Coron to Puerto Princesa in 12 Days
subtitle: Hidden lagoons, wartime wrecks, limestone cliffs and underground rivers - the complete north-to-south traverse of Palawan
climate: Best period: November to April, dry season with 26-32°C and calm seas for boat transfers. Avoid June to October (monsoon, daily heavy rainfall, disrupted maritime connections). December-February offer the best balance between weather and crowds.

title: Ultimate Philippines Grand Tour: Rice Terraces, Lagoons & Visayas in 21 Days
subtitle: From Manila to Banaue's ancient rice terraces, Palawan's limestone lagoons, the Visayas reefs, and Siargao's surf breaks — three weeks across the archipelago
climate: Best time: December to April, dry season with 26-32°C and maximum sunshine. Avoid June to October: southwest monsoon brings daily torrential rains and typhoon risk (peak August-September). May gets very hot and humid, November remains uncertain with late typhoons.

title: Poland Complete Journey: Baltic Sea to Tatra Mountains Adventure
climate: Best time: May-September, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for cities and Baltic coast. Avoid November-March (harsh cold, -5 to 5°C, frequent snow). Zakopane is a ski resort in winter; bring warm clothes for the Tatras even in summer.

title: Baltic Coast Family Road Trip: Adventures on Poland's Pomeranian Coast
climate: Best time: June-August, temperatures 18-23°C, ideal for beaches and family activities. Avoid November-March (cold, windy, rainy, many sites closed). May and September are possible but the sea remains cold (15°C) and weather unstable.

title: Secret Eastern Poland: Primeval Forests, Painted Villages and Orthodox Heritage
subtitle: 10 days from the depths of Podlachia to the Carpathian foothills, far from the beaten track
climate: Best time: May-June and September, temperatures 15-22°C, ideal for Białowieża Forest. Avoid July-August (heat and frequent storms) and December-February (severe frost, snowy roads). Bring waterproof gear year-round.

title: Polish Baltic Coast: Gdańsk, Sopot & the Teutonic Fortress
subtitle: 6 days exploring Hanseatic amber heritage, Solidarność legacy, Tri-City beaches, and the Hel Peninsula
climate: Best time: June-August, temperatures 18-22°C, maximum sunshine. Avoid November-March (cold, icy Baltic winds, grey skies). The sea remains cool even in summer (16-18°C).

title: Kraków, Tatras & Memory: Southern Poland in 7 Days
subtitle: Royal city, salt mines, Auschwitz memorial and hiking at 1,987m in the Tatra Mountains
climate: Best time: May-June and September, temperatures 15-22°C, ideal for Krakow and hiking in the Tatras. Avoid December-February (harsh cold, -5 to -10°C, heavy snow in mountains). July-August: pleasant but crowded, frequent thunderstorms at altitude.

title: Culinary Poland: From Tatra Pierogi to Baltic Herring
subtitle: 10 days tracing regional flavors from the southern Carpathians to the Hanseatic quays of Gdańsk
climate: Best time: May to September, temperatures 18–26°C, perfect for outdoor markets and terraces. Avoid November–March: harsh cold (-5 to 5°C), Tatra roads often snowbound, short daylight. Summer is ideal but book ahead (high season in Krakow and Gdansk).

title: Warsaw and Masovia: Capital, Chopin and the Vistula
subtitle: 6 days exploring Warsaw, then the Masovian countryside - Żelazowa Wola, Płock and Kazimierz Dolny along the Vistula River
climate: Best time: May-June and September, temperatures 15-22°C, ideal for exploring Warsaw and the Vistula riverside. Avoid January-February (freezing, -5 to 5°C, snow) and July-August can be muggy with thunderstorms. Fall turns rainy from October onward.

title: Greater Poland: Birthplace of the Polish State in 9 Days
subtitle: From Poznań to Kalisz via Gniezno, this 9-day circuit follows the Piast Trail to the Vistula.
climate: Best time: May-June and September, temperatures 15-23°C, ideal for castles and historical sites. Avoid December-February (frost, snow, short days) and July-August (sometimes oppressive heat, frequent thunderstorms). Spring and autumn offer stable weather for road trips.

title: Kotlina Kłodzka in 10 Days: A Loop Through Waters and Stone
subtitle: 10 days among Frederician fortresses, thousand-year spas, and the sandstone labyrinths of the Sudetes.
keywords: Srebrna Góra largest mountain fortress Europe
Góry Stołowe Szczeliniec Błędne Skały hiking
climate: Best period: May-September, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for hiking in the Sudetes. Avoid December-March (harsh winter, -5 to 5°C, heavy snow). Frequent summer thunderstorms, morning fog in valleys.

title: 8 Days in Lubusz - Fortresses, Lakes and the Oder Valley
subtitle: Medieval castles, Nazi bunkers and UNESCO parks along the Oder and Warta rivers in 8 days.
keywords: Stalag Luft III Great Escape Żagań
Łagów Knights of St John castle lakes
climate: Best time: May-September, temperatures 16-24°C, ideal for lakes and forest walks. Avoid November-March: cold and damp (0-5°C), grey skies, icy roads in winter. July-August: afternoon thunderstorms but pleasant overall.

title: From Zamość to Szydłów: Eastern Poland in 7 Days
subtitle: A road trip through the forests of Roztocze, Renaissance towns along the Vistula, and medieval ramparts of the Świętokrzyskie highlands.
climate: Best period: May-September, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for hiking and sightseeing. Avoid December-February (harsh cold, -5 to 0°C, frequent snow). Pleasant spring and fall but cool evenings.

title: West Pomerania: Szczecin, Baltic Sea & Lakes
subtitle: An 8-day road trip through Gothic brickwork, Baltic cliffs, secret forests and the glacial lakes of Pomerania.
climate: Best period: May-September, temperatures 15-22°C on the Baltic coast, ideal for beaches and lakes. Avoid November-March: harsh cold, rough seas, many sites closed. July-August: high season but changeable weather, bring a windbreaker.

title: Wrocław, Silesian Castles and the Sudetes in 12 Days
subtitle: From Poznań to Wrocław, this route crosses western Poland through Baroque abbeys, Prussian fortresses, and mountain valleys.
keywords: Churches of Peace UNESCO Jawor Świdnica
climate: Best time: May to September, temperatures 15-25°C in lowlands, cooler in mountains. Avoid November to March (cold 0 to -5°C, grey skies, short days) unless skiing in the Sudetes. Possible thunderstorms in summer.

title: Poland: Lower Silesia and Greater Poland in 6 days
subtitle: Travel from Wrocław to Poznań through castles, abbeys, and wooden churches of Silesia and Greater Poland.
climate: Best period: May-September, temperatures 18-25°C, long sunny days. Avoid December-February (frequent frost, -5 to 2°C, very short days). Spring and autumn pleasant but cooler (10-15°C).

title: Poland: From Wrocław to the Holy Cross Mountains in 6 Days
subtitle: Link Wrocław and its dwarves to Częstochowa, the Eagles' Nests castles, and the Holy Cross Mountains.
climate: Best time: May-June and September, temperatures 15-23°C, low rainfall. Avoid July-August (frequent storms, heat up to 30°C) and December-February (frost, snow, short days). Spring ideal for Jura castles and Świętokrzyskie forests.

title: Alentejo UNESCO Heritage: Évora, Elvas and Medieval Villages
climate: Best period: March-May and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C ideal for heritage sites and medieval villages. Avoid June-August: extreme heat regularly exceeding 38°C, little shade. Mild winter (10-15°C) but rainy, some sites less accessible.

title: Beira Interior Road Trip, Portugal: Serra da Estrela, Schist Villages & History
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C at altitude, 20-28°C in valleys. Avoid December-March (snow at elevation, road closures to Sabugueiro, severe cold). Summer hot in lowlands (35°C+), but pleasant in the mountains.

title: Portugal Discovery Tour: From Lisbon to Alentejo Plains
climate: Best period: April-June and September-October, temperatures 18-28°C. Avoid July-August: scorching heat in inland Alentejo (35-40°C), though Aveiro coast stays cooler. Covilha (mountain) can be cold in winter with possible snow.

title: Lisbon and Sintra Escape: City Views, Romantic Palaces and Coastal Cliffs
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 18-25°C, ideal for visiting palaces and gardens. Avoid July-August (intense heat 30-35°C, tourist crowds). Mild winters (12-15°C) but rainy, especially in Sintra.

title: Portugal Grand Tour: Algarve Coast to Northern Mountain Peaks
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, with 18-25°C and little rain. Avoid July-August in the Algarve (intense heat 35-40°C, crowds) and December-February in the north (continuous rain, cooler temps). Porto and Gerês stay cooler and wetter year-round than the south.

title: Portugal North Bound: Lisbon to Peneda-Gerês National Park
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for this diverse route. Avoid July-August (scorching heat in Douro Valley, crowds in Lisbon). The north (Gerês, Viana) gets significantly more rain from October to March.

title: Portugal's Trás-os-Montes: Bragança, Douro Valley & Montesinho Park
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-27°C, perfect for exploring vineyards and heritage sites. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 35-40°C in the Douro Valley). Winter is cold with possible frost at higher elevations in Trás-os-Montes.

title: Inland Alentejo to the Wild Vicentine Coast
subtitle: Marble villages, medieval castles and untamed Atlantic cliffs in 5 days
climate: Best period: April-May and September-October, 20-28°C inland, cooler on the coast (18-24°C). Avoid July-August: scorching heat in the interior Alentejo (38-42°C), though the coast stays breezy. Mild but rainy winters.

title: Portugal: Historic Villages & Serra da Estrela in 8 Days
subtitle: From Castelo Branco to frontier fortresses: 12 medieval villages and the roof of Portugal.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C in villages, cooler at altitude in Serra da Estrela. Avoid July-August (scorching heat in valleys, up to 35°C) and December-February (snow at Torre and mountains, roads sometimes closed). High day-night temperature variations at altitude.

title: Lisbon and Upper Alentejo Fortresses in 8 Days
subtitle: From the capital to the Raia ridges: 8 days through Lisbon, Marvão, and Castelo de Vide.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 18-28°C, ideal for exploring Lisbon and the fortresses. Avoid July-August: scorching heat in Alentejo (35-40°C), little shade in fortified villages. Mild winter (10-15°C) but rainy.

title: Border Alentejo: Guadiana and Alqueva in 7 Days
subtitle: From Mértola's Roman mines to Monsaraz's Templar ramparts, the Portugal that faces Spain.
climate: Best period: March-May and September-October, temperatures 18-28°C, ideal for exploring Alqueva and villages. Avoid July-August: scorching heat of 38-42°C in inland Alentejo, virtually no rain. Mild winter (10-15°C) but cool nights along the Guadiana.

title: The Grand Lisbon and Southern Portugal Tour in 15 Days
subtitle: From Lisbon to the Sagres cliffs via Arrábida, then inland return through Mértola, Serpa and Beja.
climate: Best period: April-May and September-October, temperatures 18-26°C, generous sunshine. Avoid July-August in inland Alentejo (Beja, Mértola) where temperatures regularly exceed 38°C. Atlantic coast windy year-round, bring a windbreaker even in summer.

title: Paraguay: Chaco Expedition, Mennonite Colonies & Untamed Wilderness
subtitle: 11 days crossing the Paraguayan Gran Chaco from the capital to the Bolivian borderlands, through Mennonite history, Chaco War memory, and extraordinary wildlife.
climate: Best time: May to September (austral winter), daytime temperatures 20-28°C, cool nights. Strictly avoid November-March: extreme heat (40-48°C), grueling roads, high health risk. The Chaco is one of South America's hottest regions.

title: Paraguay: Jesuit Missions, Red Earth and Río Paraguay
subtitle: From UNESCO-listed Jesuit ruins to the banks of the Río Paraguay, crossing Guaraní craftsmanship, Iguazú Falls, and the red earth landscapes of the heart of South America.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-November, temperatures 20-28°C with lower humidity. Avoid December-February (oppressive heat 35-40°C, high humidity). Rain possible year-round but moderate in spring-fall.

title: Paraguay: Jesuit Missions, Waterfalls & Red Earth in 11 Days
subtitle: From the colonial capital to UNESCO-listed Jesuit ruins, via Iguazú Falls viewed from three countries.
climate: Best time: April-May and August-October, temperatures 20-28°C, ideal for visiting missions and waterfalls. Avoid December-February (scorching heat 35-40°C, high humidity, frequent storms). Winter (June-July) is mild but daylight hours are shorter in subtropical areas.

title: Réunion Island Circuit: Cirques, Volcanoes & Beaches
subtitle: Clockwise loop from Saint-Denis: three UNESCO-listed cirques, France's most active volcano, and the turquoise lagoon of the west coast.
climate: Best time: May to October (austral winter), 20-26°C on the coast, 12-20°C in the cirques, dry and sunny. Avoid January to March: cyclone season, torrential rains in the highlands, oppressive humid heat (28-32°C). The volcano is accessible year-round but hike during the dry season.

title: Romania Black Sea Itinerary: Tomis, Constanța and Coastal Heritage
climate: Best period: May-June and September, temperatures 20-28°C, ideal for archaeological sites and enjoying the Black Sea. Avoid July-August (intense heat up to 35°C, crowded beaches) and November-March (cold, icy wind from the Black Sea, some sites closed).

title: Romania Road Trip: Transylvania, Bucovina & Maramureș
subtitle: 10 days exploring royal castles, UNESCO-painted monasteries, and villages frozen in time
climate: Best time: May-June and September, temperatures 15-25°C in the Carpathians, ideal for sightseeing. Avoid July-August (plains heat up to 35°C in Bucharest, frequent mountain thunderstorms) and December-March (snow-covered roads, mountain passes closed in Maramureș). Pack warm clothes even in summer for higher altitudes.

title: Transylvania 12 days: Dracula, Merry Cemetery & Gothic Castles
subtitle: An 12 days loop from Cluj-Napoca, from the wooden churches of Maramureș to Gothic castles and Saxon citadels.
keywords: Mocănița steam train Vișeu de Sus
climate: Best time: May-June and September, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for castles and wooden churches. Avoid December-March (heavy snow, difficult mountain roads, -5 to 5°C) and July-August (heat up to 30°C, frequent storms). The Carpathians bring significant temperature variations between valleys and mountain passes.

title: Wild Banat, Mountains & Danube: 8-Day Road Trip
subtitle: From Timișoara's baroque squares to the Danube cliffs: 8 days through mountains, waterfalls, and Roman ruins.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 18-25°C, ideal for hiking and waterfalls. Avoid July-August (intense heat in Danube gorges, up to 35°C) and December-March (mountain snow, difficult road access). Spring is rainy but waterfalls are spectacular.

title: Crișana & Apuseni: Caves, Vineyards & Art Nouveau, 8 days
subtitle: From the Arad plain to the Apuseni mountains, an 8-day road trip through fortresses, ice caves and thermal waters.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 18-26°C in the plains, 15-20°C in the mountains. Avoid July-August (scorching heat in Arad and Oradea, frequent storms in the Apuseni) and December-March (harsh cold, difficult cave access, snow on mountain roads). Vineyards and caves are best visited in spring and early autumn.

title: Romania: Transylvania & Transfăgărășan in 10 days
subtitle: Link Bucharest to Saxon fortified churches, Sighișoara, and the legendary Transfăgărășan behind the wheel.
climate: Best period: May-June and September, 15-25°C, Transfagarasan road open. Avoid October to June: pass closed by snow. July-August: hot in lowlands (up to 30°C), cool at altitude, high tourist traffic.

title: Romania: Oltenia, the Danube and the Iron Gates in 6 Days
subtitle: Follow the Danube and the Carpathian foothills, from Brâncovenesc monasteries to the Iron Gates gorges.

title: Parks, Canyons and Monasteries of Southwest Serbia
subtitle: An 11-day route through the heart of Serbia, from Šumadija to the eastern spas.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, with temperatures from 20 to 26°C in valleys, cooler in mountains (Zlatibor, Kopaonik). Avoid July-August (intense heat up to 35°C in lowlands) and December-March (snow and difficult mountain roads). Spring is ideal for monasteries and national parks.

title: Vojvodina and Iron Gates: 13 days Serbian road trip
subtitle: From Belgrade to the Danube gorges, an 13 days circuit through monasteries, Vojvodina vineyards, and Roman fortresses.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 18-26°C, pleasant light. Avoid July-August (scorching heat in the plains, up to 35-40°C) and December-February (cold, dense fog in Vojvodina). Spring and autumn offer ideal conditions for sightseeing and cruising along the Danube.

title: Rwanda: 13-Day Loop Through Gorillas, Lakes & Volcanoes
subtitle: This 13-day road trip sweeps across Rwanda from Akagera to the Virunga volcanoes, via Nyungwe and Lake Kivu.
climate: Best period: June-September and December-February, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for gorilla trekking. Avoid March-May (heavy rains, muddy trails in Nyungwe and Volcanoes forests). Evenings are cool at altitude, bring layers.

title: Wild Central Sweden: Dalarna, Härjedalen & Jämtland
subtitle: From Mora to Åre's peaks: lakes, fells and glaciers in the heart of the Swedish wilderness
climate: Best time: June-August for hiking (15-22°C, long daylight), December-March for skiing (Åre and Sälen resorts). Avoid April-May (snowmelt, muddy roads) and November (near-constant darkness, cold without snow). Harsh winters with -15 to -25°C, prepare for extreme cold.

title: The Grand South: From Malmö to Stockholm via the Islands
subtitle: 10 days exploring Baltic coastlines, medieval islands, and the Swedish capital
climate: Best time: May to September, temperatures 15-23°C, long summer days (19 hours of daylight in June). Avoid November to March: cold (often below freezing), 18-hour nights, frequent snow. Gotland enjoys a slightly milder microclimate.

title: Scandinavia Road Trip: Stockholm to the Lofoten Islands
subtitle: From the Arctic night train to fishermen's cabins in the Lofoten, 14 days between Sweden and Norway beyond the polar circle
climate: Best time: June-August, temperatures 15-20°C in Stockholm, 10-15°C in Lapland and Lofoten, with midnight sun above the Arctic Circle. Avoid October-April: partial polar night, severe cold (-20°C in Abisko), snow-covered roads in Lapland. For northern lights, choose September or March, but expect cold (-5 to -15°C).

title: The Great Northern Journey: Stockholm to Lapland via the East Coast
subtitle: 17 days by campervan or car along the Baltic, from the Swedish capital to the Arctic Circle and the gateway to Abisko
climate: Best period: June to August, with 18-25°C in the south and 10-18°C in Lapland, midnight sun from mid-June to mid-July beyond the Arctic Circle. Avoid October to April: harsh winter with freezing temperatures, snow-covered roads, and polar night in the north. Beware of abundant mosquitoes in Lapland during June-July.

title: Slovenia's Highlights: Lake Bled, Ljubljana, Piran & Postojna Cave
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C in the mountains, up to 28°C on the coast. Avoid July-August (coastal heat, crowds) and December-March (Alpine passes closed, snow in the Julian Alps). Postojna caves stay at 10°C year-round.

title: Slovak Treasures: High Tatras, Castles and Medieval Town Lanes
climate: Best period: May-June and September, temperatures 18-25°C. Avoid July-August (frequent thunderstorms, humid heat up to 30°C) and December-February (freezing conditions, heavy snow in the Tatras, difficult roads). Bring warm clothing for mountain areas even in summer.

title: Slovakia: Castles, Wooden Villages and Mines in 11 Days
subtitle: From Trenčín to Topoľčany, 11 days linking medieval fortresses, painted villages and silver mines.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-24°C, ideal for castles and villages. Avoid July-August (severe mountain thunderstorms) and December-February (harsh cold, snowy roads). Pack warm clothes for higher elevations.

title: Complete El Salvador Road Trip: From the Country's Only UNESCO Site to the Morazán Mountains
subtitle: 10 days from San Salvador to western volcanoes, the Pacific coast, coffee villages, and civil war memorials

san andres maya site el salvador

title: Essential El Salvador Circuit: Flower Route and the Pacific
subtitle: San Salvador, Santa Ana volcanoes, coffee villages, and surf beaches in one week

climate: Best time: November to April (dry season), 25-30°C, perfect for the Flower Route and Pacific beaches. Avoid May-October: heavy afternoon downpours, humid heat (28-32°C), rough seas. Hurricane risk June-November on the coast.

title: Central Thailand: Bangkok, Ancient Kingdoms & the River Kwai
subtitle: From Bangkok's gilded temples to Ayutthaya's haunting ruins, from the Bridge on the River Kwai to Erawan's turquoise cascades - 14 days through the heart of historic Thailand.

subtitle: 12 days of golden temples facing the sea, boat-only beaches and some of the richest underwater life in the Gulf of Thailand.

subtitle: 13 days on the road through Lanna temples, dizzying mountain passes, and the birthplace of the first Thai kingdom
climate: Best period: November to February, temperatures 20-28°C (cool mountain nights, 10-15°C). Avoid March-April (scorching heat up to 40°C + agricultural burning pollution) and June-October (monsoon, daily rains). Mountain roads can be slippery during rainy season.

subtitle: From Hua Hin to the Malaysian border: 14 days along the Andaman coast — from Khao Sok's jungle to Koh Lipe's crystal waters.
climate: Best time: November to March, dry season with 28-32°C and calm seas. Avoid May-October: heavy monsoon on Andaman coast, daily downpours, rough seas and some islands inaccessible. High humidity year-round.

title: Essential Thailand: Temples, Jungle & Beaches in 2 Weeks
subtitle: From Bangkok to the Andaman Islands via Chiang Mai and Chiang Rai: the complete Thailand circuit for first-time visitors.
keywords: thailand circuit bangkok chiang mai phuket
climate: Best period: November to February, temperatures 25-30°C, clear skies. Avoid April-May (scorching heat up to 38°C) and June-October (monsoon season, daily rains, rough seas in the south). December-January ideal for combining north and beaches.

title: Secret Thailand: Isan, Koh Chang & Forgotten Parks
subtitle: 14 days far from the classic routes: Bangkok street art, Khao Yai jungle, forgotten Khmer temples, the Isan plateau and the wild eastern coast.
climate: Best time: November to February, temperatures 20-28°C, dry and pleasant in Isan. Avoid March-May (scorching heat 35-40°C in the northeast) and July-September (heavy monsoon on Koh Chang). Isan experiences significant temperature swings in winter.

title: Turkmenistan Circuit: Ashgabat, Karakum Desert and the Caspian Sea
subtitle: From the marble capital to the flames of Darvaza, from medieval Silk Road cities to the Caspian coast
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, temperatures 18-28°C, dry climate perfect for desert travel. Avoid June-August (extreme heat 40-45°C in Karakum, unbearable). Winter is cold with icy winds, not recommended.

title: Turkish Aegean Coast: Gallipoli, Troy, Ephesus & Bodrum in 12 Days
subtitle: From the Gallipoli Peninsula to the ancient cities of Ionia, a slow journey through battlefields, UNESCO sites, and fishing villages down to the Bodrum Peninsula
climate: Best period: April-May and September-October, temperatures 20-28°C, ideal for archaeological sites. Avoid July-August: scorching heat (35-40°C), ruins in full sun unbearable. Mild but rainy winter along the Aegean coast.

title: Trabzon to Istanbul: Black Sea, Hittites, and Safranbolu in 10 Days
subtitle: Cliff-hanging monasteries, terraced tea plantations, Hittite capitals, and Ottoman townhouses - the complete northern Turkey loop
climate: Best period: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C. Avoid July-August (scorching heat in Central Anatolia, up to 35°C) and winter (frequent snow in Safranbolu and Bogazkale, constant rain on the Black Sea coast). The Trabzon-Rize area remains humid year-round: bring a raincoat even in summer.

title: White cotton, whirling dervishes and fairy chimneys - 10 days from Antalya to Cappadocia
subtitle: Pamukkale, Aphrodisias, Konya, Sultanhanı, Ihlara and Göreme: Central Anatolia at your own pace
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, clear skies for hot air balloons in Cappadocia. Avoid July-August (scorching heat in Konya and Pamukkale, 35-40°C) and December-February (harsh frost in Cappadocia, possible snow). Large day-night temperature swings at altitude.

title: Turkey End to End: The Complete 25-Day Grand Tour
subtitle: 5 regions, 1 loop, 25 days to see it all - from Istanbul to the far reaches of Eastern Anatolia
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 20-30°C on the coast, 15-25°C in the mountains. Avoid July-August (scorching heat, 35-40°C) and November-April (snow in Eastern Anatolia, roads closed to Van and Kars). This route requires the summer season.

title: Istanbul in 6 days: from sultans to the Bosphorus, neighbourhood by neighbourhood
subtitle: An explorer's circuit placing every major site on the map, condensable to 5–10 days
climate: Best period: April-May and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for walking between monuments. Avoid July-August (heavy heat up to 35°C, massive crowds) and December-February (frequent rain, 5-10°C, occasional snow). Spring offers the finest light over the Bosphorus.

title: Gaziantep to Trabzon: Mesopotamia, Nemrut & Historic Armenia - 14 Days
subtitle: Journey through forgotten civilizations across Upper Mesopotamia, the Syriac Tigris, and the Armenian highlands
climate: Best period: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-28°C. Avoid July-August (extreme heat 40-45°C in the Southeast) and November-April (heavy snow at altitude, closed mountain passes, -15°C possible in Kars/Erzurum). Significant elevation changes: bring warm clothing even in summer.

title: From Caria to Pamphylia: 12 Days on the Turquoise Coast, Marmaris to Antalya
subtitle: Slow travel road trip along the Lycian Way - cliff tombs, sunken cities, eternal flames and blue lagoons between Bodrum and Antalya
climate: Best period: April-June and September-October, temperatures 20-28°C, sea 22-25°C. Avoid July-August (intense heat 35-40°C, massive crowds) and December-March (frequent rain, muddy archaeological sites). Boat trips and Lycian Way hiking are ideal in spring.

title: Secret Central Turkey: 7 Days Ankara to Kayseri
subtitle: Journey through eight stages from the Hittite capital to Amasya's rock tombs, the madrasas of Sivas, and UNESCO-listed Divriği.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-28°C, ideal for cultural touring. Avoid July-August (intense dry heat, 35-40°C) and December-March (harsh winters, frequent snow in Sivas). Large day-night temperature swings: bring layers.

subtitle: Over 13 days, you cross the Turkish highlands from Ani to Lake Van and the black ramparts of Diyarbakır.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C at altitude. Avoid winter (November-March): severe frost in Erzurum and Kars (-20°C), mountain passes closed, roads impassable. July-August: scorching heat in Diyarbakir (35-40°C), tolerable at higher elevations near Mount Ararat.

title: Turkish Black Sea Road Trip: 8 Days from Istanbul to Hopa
subtitle: Trace Turkey's forgotten northern coast, from Safranbolu's UNESCO Ottoman houses all the way to the Georgian border.
climate: Best period: May-June and September-October, temperatures 20-26°C with decent sunshine. Avoid July-August (oppressive humid heat, thunderstorms) and November-March (continuous rain along the coast, frequent fog). The Rize-Hopa area gets 2200 mm/year: pack waterproofs even in summer.

title: Extended Cappadocia in 8 Days: Kayseri, Göreme, Konya
subtitle: Traverse Cappadocia over 8 days, from the fairy chimneys of Göreme to the whirling dervishes of Konya.

title: Turkish Mesopotamia: 11 Days from Gaziantep to the Tigris
subtitle: Cross Southeast Anatolia in 11 days, from the baklava capital to the black ramparts of Diyarbakır.

title: 7-Day Taurus: Turquoise Lakes & Lycian Cities
subtitle: In 7 days, journey from Antalya to Eğirdir, through Sagalassos and Lake Salda, ending in Fethiye.
climate: Best period: April-May and September-October, daytime temperatures 20-28°C. Avoid July-August (scorching heat, 35-40°C inland, archaeological sites unbearable at midday). Mountain lakes stay cool, possible frost in winter.

title: Black Sea & Safranbolu: 10 days village to village
subtitle: 10 days from Istanbul to the lakes of Bolu via the Black Sea coast and Safranbolu's Ottoman houses.
climate: Best time: May-June and September, temperatures 18-25°C on the coast, cooler in the mountains (Bolu, Yedigoller). Avoid November-March: heavy rainfall on the Black Sea coast, snow and difficult roads in the mountains. Summer (July-August) tolerable but humid along the coast.

title: Istanbul to Sofia: Edirne, Plovdiv, Rila & the Rhodopes
subtitle: A 10-day road trip crossing Ottoman Turkey and the Bulgarian Rhodopes to the monasteries of Rila.
climate: Best period: May-June and September-October, temperatures 18-25°C in lowlands, cooler in mountains. High temperature variation between Istanbul, Bulgarian plains and Rila peaks.

title: Istanbul to Varna: Strandja and Black Sea Coast in 9 days
subtitle: This 9 days road trip connects Istanbul to Varna, threading through the longoz forests of Thrace, wild Strandja, and Bulgaria's Black Sea shoreline.
climate: Best period: May-June and September, temperatures 20-26°C, pleasant sea. Avoid July-August (intense heat 30-35°C, crowded beaches) and November-March (frequent rain, cold sea). Possible northeast wind in winter on the Bulgarian coast.

title: Central Anatolia: Phrygia, Konya, and the Caravanserais
subtitle: This 13 days circuit links UNESCO-listed Gordion with the whirling dervishes of Konya in the heart of Phrygian-Seljuk Anatolia.

title: The Lycian Coast: Dalyan to Antalya in 14 Days
subtitle: A road trip through sunken cities, protected turtle beaches, and mountains of fire along Turkey's Turquoise Coast.
climate: Best period: April–June and September–October, temperatures 20–28°C, pleasant sea. Avoid July–August (scorching heat 35–40°C at archaeological sites). Mild winter (15°C) but frequent rain; some mountain roads may be closed.

title: Tanzania: Northern Circuit Through Volcanoes, Craters, and Savanna

title: Tanzania Safari & Zanzibar: Northern Circuit and Indian Ocean Beaches
subtitle: From the Serengeti to the reefs of Zanzibar
climate: Best time: June-October (dry season), 20-28°C, ideal for safari and beaches. Avoid March-May (heavy rains, muddy park roads). Cool nights in Ngorongoro (15°C), high humidity in Zanzibar year-round.

subtitle: From Denali's vast tundra plains to Fairbanks' northern lights: six days deep in Alaska's wild interior.

title: Appalachian Road Trip: Great Smoky Mountains & Blue Ridge
subtitle: A leisurely mountain loop through mist-shrouded peaks, artisan villages, and winding highland roads
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, temperatures 60-75°F, ideal for spectacular fall foliage. Avoid July-August (humid heat 85°F+, frequent mountain thunderstorms). Cold winters at higher elevations in Boone and Great Smoky Mountains, occasional snow on roads.

title: Arizona Grand Loop: Grand Canyon and Desert Highways
subtitle: From Phoenix to the Grand Canyon via Tucson, the Petrified Forest, and Sedona - nine days through Arizona's most legendary landscapes.

Significant temperature variation between desert and plateaus.

title: Arkansas Itinerary: Hot Springs, Ozarks and Natural Parks
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 65-77°F, ideal for hiking the Ozarks. Avoid July-August: oppressive heat (86-95°F), high humidity, and frequent thunderstorms. Mild winter but unpredictable, possible freezing in the mountains.

title: California Road Trip: San Francisco, Yosemite & Death Valley
subtitle: 18 days from San Francisco to Los Angeles through Yosemite, Death Valley, and Joshua Tree National Parks, returning via the Pacific Coast.
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, with temperatures ranging from 15 to 30°C depending on the area. Avoid Death Valley and Joshua Tree from June to September (45-50°C, life-threatening). Yosemite partly closed in winter (snow), expect coastal fog in summer around San Francisco.

title: Driving the Pacific Coast Highway: A Golden Gate Bridge to Hollywood Road Trip
climate: Best time: April-June and September-November, temps 60-75°F, clear skies. Avoid July-August (frequent coastal fog, especially around San Francisco and Big Sur). Mild winters but Highway 1 may close at Big Sur after heavy rains.

title: Baltimore to the Outer Banks - Historic America from Chesapeake Bay to Charlotte
subtitle: This 8-stop journey traces the thread of American history, from Baltimore's shipyards to the sand dunes of the Outer Banks, through Virginia's colonial capitals and Atlantic beaches.
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, temperatures 64-77°F, moderate humidity. Avoid July-August (oppressive heat 86-95°F, stifling humidity, thunderstorms) and August-October on the Outer Banks (hurricane season). Mild winters in the Carolinas, colder in Baltimore.

title: Black Hills & Badlands: Road Trip Through the Heart of the American West
subtitle: From the geological desert of the Badlands to the granite forests of the Black Hills, this 7-day circuit connects 75-million-year-old fossils, gold rush legends, and monuments carved into rock.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for hiking. Avoid July-August (scorching heat in the Badlands up to 38°C, severe thunderstorms) and December-March (freezing cold -10 to 0°C, mountain roads closed). The Black Hills offer some summer relief but remain exposed to storms.

title: Boston, Maine & the Islands: 10 Days Through New England
subtitle: From Colonial Boston to Nantucket's cobblestones, this journey crosses American history, Acadia's forests, and the waters of the North Atlantic.
keywords: what to see new england 10 days
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 59-77°F, with spectacular fall foliage in late September. Avoid January-March (harsh winter, snow, below freezing) and July-August (humid heat, crowded islands). Coastal areas stay breezy even in summer, bring a windbreaker.

title: Colorado-Wyoming Road Trip: National Parks, Rockies & Wide Open Spaces
subtitle: This two-week circuit connects Denver to Wyoming's Rockies before diving into Colorado's canyons and dunes.
keywords: rocky mountain national park trail ridge road
climate: Best time: June-September, temperatures 15-25°C in valleys, cooler at elevation. Avoid winter (mountain passes closed, -10 to -20°C) and July-August if concerned about violent afternoon thunderstorms. High altitude: cool nights even in summer.

title: Minneapolis, Voyageurs, Isle Royale & the Upper Peninsula
subtitle: This route crosses the American North, from the urban lakes of Minneapolis to Michigan's remote forests, passing through two national parks accessible only by boat.
climate: Best time: June through September, temperatures 18–26°C, ideal for hiking and boating. Avoid November–March: arctic winters (-15 to -30°C), heavy lake-effect snow in the Upper Peninsula. International Falls is one of the coldest cities in the continental United States.

title: Louisville, Mammoth Cave & Congaree - Caves and Swamps of the Deep South
subtitle: This seven-day road trip links the world bourbon capital to the longest known cave system, before plunging into South Carolina's old-growth floodplain forests.
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, temperatures 18-26°C with moderate humidity. Avoid June-August: stifling heat (30-35°C) and extreme humidity, frequent storms in caves and swamps. Mild but rainy winter in South Carolina.

title: Salt Lake City to Great Basin Road Trip: Desert, Salt & Stars
subtitle: This 6-day road trip crosses Utah and Nevada via the Bonneville Salt Flats, mining towns along US-50, and Great Basin National Park.
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, clear skies ideal for stargazing at Great Basin. Avoid June-August (extreme desert heat, up to 38°C in Wendover) and December-February (severe cold, snow at elevation). Cool nights year-round, even in summer.

title: Ozarks & Branson: From Missouri Hills to Arkansas Art Galleries
subtitle: This five-day road trip descends from the Missouri Ozarks to northwest Arkansas, passing through a live entertainment capital, a Victorian village frozen in time, and a free art museum set among oak trees.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 18-25°C (64-77°F), low humidity. Avoid July-August (oppressive heat, daily thunderstorms). Winters are mild but rainy in the Ozarks.

title: Alaska Adventure - 14 Days Through the World's Most Remote National Parks
subtitle: This flight-based circuit connects six Arctic and sub-Arctic national parks across Alaska, from Katmai's salmon-fishing bears to the polar sand dunes of Kobuk Valley.
climate: Best period: June-August, 10-20°C, very long days. Only window for remote parks and inter-park flights. Avoid September-May (extreme cold, no access). Frequent rain in southeast, mosquitoes in July.

title: Wild Arizona: Tucson, Tombstone & Bisbee
subtitle: This three-day circuit crosses southern Arizona, from the Sonoran Desert to the copper mines of the Mule Mountains, passing through the most famous street in the Wild West.
climate: Best time: October to April, temperatures 15-25°C (59-77°F), nearly constant sunshine. Avoid June-August (extreme desert heat, 40-43°C/104-109°F). Summer monsoon July-September brings violent late-afternoon thunderstorms.

title: 8-Day New England Road Trip: Boston, Salem, Cape Cod, Newport & Providence
subtitle: From Boston to Providence, this 8-day circuit traverses colonial America, the 1692 witch trials, Cape Cod's wild dunes, and the Vanderbilt palaces of the Gilded Age.
keywords: boston salem cape cod newport road trip
climate: Best time: May-October, temperatures 18-26°C. September-early October ideal for fall foliage and fewer crowds. Avoid November-April (harsh winters, frequent snow, many attractions closed).

title: 8 Days in Louisiana: New Orleans and the Land of Voodoo
subtitle: From the voodoo-haunted French Quarter to cypress bayous, this 8-day circuit crosses Louisiana between slavery memory, Creole architecture, Cajun music, and alligators in the swamps.
climate: Best time: March-May and October-November, temperatures 20-28°C. Avoid June-September at all costs (sweltering heat 32-35°C, oppressive humidity, hurricane risk). Mild but humid winters, frequent rain.

title: 10-Day American Far West: San Francisco, Yosemite, Bodie Ghost Town, Death Valley, Las Vegas & Grand Canyon
subtitle: From San Francisco to the Grand Canyon in 10 days, this road trip crosses coastal California, the Sierra Nevada, ghost towns, Death Valley, and the Las Vegas Strip before ending at 1,800 meters of exposed geology.
keywords: far west california nevada road trip 10 days
death valley racetrack playa moving stones
death valley national park road trip
climate: Best period: April-May and September-October, temperatures 60-82°F depending on elevation. Avoid June-August at all costs: Death Valley is deadly (115-120°F), Las Vegas unbearable. Winter impractical: Tioga Pass closed, mountain roads snow-covered until May.

title: 9-Day Pacific Northwest: Uncovering Hidden Gems and Cult Locations
subtitle: From Seattle to Portland over 9 days, this road trip crosses the misty Pacific Northwest between Twin Peaks pilgrimage, Olympic Peninsula rainforests, wild Oregon coast, and the still-active Mount St. Helens volcano.
keywords: seattle to portland twin peaks circuit
oregon dunes florence frank herbert dune
twin peaks filming locations snoqualmie washington
climate: Best time: June-September, 18-25°C, clear skies. Avoid November-March: constant rain (200+ days/year), persistent gray skies, reduced coastal visibility. Dry summer, very rainy winter typical of oceanic climate.

title: California Wine Country in 10 Days: San Francisco to Los Angeles Through the Vineyards
subtitle: This 10-day circuit connects San Francisco, Napa Valley, Sonoma, Paso Robles, and Santa Ynez Valley to traverse California wine history, from the Cabernet that beat Bordeaux to the Pinot immortalized by Sideways.
climate: Best time: April-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, perfect for wine tasting. Avoid July-August in inland valleys (Napa, Paso Robles): intense heat 35-40°C. Coast stays cool year-round with frequent summer fog.

title: From the Berkshires to Niagara Falls: 10 Days of Waterfalls, Villages & Wilderness
subtitle: A 10-day journey connecting the hills of Massachusetts to the Canadian border via Saratoga, Cooperstown, the Finger Lakes, and two of America's most stunning state parks.
climate: Best time: May-June (waterfalls at peak flow, 59-72°F) and September-October (spectacular fall foliage, 54-68°F). Avoid November-March: heavy snow across upstate New York, road closures, frequent freezing temperatures. July-August workable but hot and humid (77-86°F), heavy crowds at Niagara Falls.

title: Boston to Niagara Falls in 13 Days, Through New England and New York State
subtitle: A 13-day journey connecting Boston and Niagara, alternating historic cities, Berkshires villages, Olympic lakes, and Finger Lakes gorges.
climate: Best time: May-June and September-October, temperatures 15-25°C, ideal for scenery and hiking. Avoid July-August (humid heat 30°C+, frequent thunderstorms) and December-March (heavy snow, harsh cold -5 to 5°C, difficult roads in the Adirondacks). Fall offers New England's famous foliage colors.

title: Jacksonville to Dallas in 12 Days Through the Deep South
subtitle: A 12-day cross-country journey linking Florida to Texas through turquoise springs, Civil Rights landmarks, Mississippi River mansions, and colonial Louisiana.
climate: Best time: March-May and October-November, temperatures 18-28°C with moderate humidity. Avoid July-August (brutal heat 32-38°C, oppressive humidity, severe thunderstorms) and hurricane season (June-November, peak August-October). Winter is mild (10-18°C) but rainy.

title: Washington to Miami in 16 Days Along the Atlantic Coast
subtitle: A 16-day journey from the federal capital to Miami, tracing the Atlantic seaboard through colonial towns, wild barrier islands, and space launch pads.
climate: Best time: April-May and October-November, temperatures 68-79°F with lower humidity. Avoid June-September: oppressive heat (86-95°F), extreme humidity, and hurricane season (peak August-October in Florida). North Carolina's Outer Banks can be windy in spring.

title: Uruguay: 16-day grand loop, coast and pampa
subtitle: This 16-day road trip connects Montevideo to Punta del Este through wild beaches, vineyards, and gaucho country.
climate: Best time: March-May and October-November, temperatures 18-25°C, ideal for beaches and pampas. Avoid December-February (heavy heat up to 35°C, tourist crowds on the coast). Winter June-August is feasible but cool (10-16°C) and humid, not recommended for coastal visits.

title: Uzbekistan: From Tashkent to the Fergana Valley Along the Silk Road
subtitle: Timurid capitals, Tian Shan mountains and the kingdom of ikat silk
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, temperatures 20-28°C, clear skies. Avoid July-August (scorching heat 40°C+ in Bukhara and Samarkand deserts, bone-dry). Winter is cold (-5 to 5°C), spring brief but ideal for the Silk Road.

title: Essential Vietnam: North, Center and South in 12 Days
subtitle: Vietnam's unmissable highlights in under two weeks - from Ha Long Bay to the Mekong Delta
keywords: ha long bay junk cruise overnight
ninh binh tam coc boat tour
climate: Best time: February to April, temperatures 22-30°C, ideal across all three regions. Avoid September-January (heavy monsoon in central Vietnam, Hue and Hoi An often flooded) and May-August (scorching heat 35-38°C, high humidity). December-February: warm in the south but cool in the north (15-20°C in Hanoi).

title: Grand Tour of Vietnam: From Hanoi to the Mekong Delta in 21 Days
subtitle: Northern rice paddies, Central imperial cities, and Southern river life - Vietnam's full diversity revealed
keywords: Pu Luong terraced rice fields Thai villages
climate: Best time: February-April (dry throughout, 20-28°C) or November-early December (ideal north and south, riskier in central). Avoid June-September (northern rains, central typhoons) and October-January in central regions (continuous rain in Hue/Hoi An). The delta remains accessible year-round but expect daily showers May-October.

title: Northeast Vietnam Loop: Ha Giang, Ban Gioc & Ba Be
subtitle: Vietnam at its wildest - Dong Van geopark, frontier waterfalls, and a lake lost in the jungle
climate: Best time: September-November and March-April, temperatures 15-25°C, clear roads and stunning views. Avoid June-August (monsoon, muddy and dangerous roads) and December-January (cold, dense fog, frost at altitude). Pack warm clothes even in season: mountain passes exceed 2000m.

title: Northern Vietnam Circuit: Rice Terraces, Mountains, and Ha Long Bay
subtitle: From Hanoi's streets to Sapa's terraces and the karsts of the bay - the deep North in 11 days
climate: Best time: October-November and March-April, temperatures 20-28°C, dry weather perfect for rice terraces and trekking. Avoid June-September (monsoon, heavy rains, typhoons in Ha Long) and December-February (cold in Sapa 5-10°C, thick mountain fog). Pack warm clothes for Sapa even in dry season.

title: Vietnam's Central Highlands: Coffee, Ethnic Villages & Dalat Waterfalls
subtitle: Vietnam's hidden face - Rong-house villages, coffee plantations, and Dalat waterfalls
climate: Best period: November to March, temperatures 18-25°C (cooler in Dalat, 15-22°C). Avoid June-September: heavy monsoon, difficult mountain roads, poor visibility. Cool nights year-round at altitude (12-15°C).

title: Southern Vietnam Circuit: Saigon, Mekong Delta & Phu Quoc
subtitle: From the pulse of Saigon through the waterways of the delta to Phu Quoc's tropical beaches
climate: Best time: November to February, temperatures 25-30°C, dry and sunny. Avoid May-October (monsoon season, heavy daily rainfall) and March-April (scorching heat up to 38°C, high humidity). Phu Quoc gets less rain than the Delta.

title: Central Vietnam Route: Imperial Cities, Lanterns & Secret Beaches
subtitle: From Hue's citadel to Quy Nhon's shores via the ancient streets of Hoi An
climate: Best time: February to April, temperatures 25-30°C, dry and sunny. Avoid September to December: heavy monsoon rains, frequent typhoons, flooded roads. May to August remains doable despite heat and humidity.

title: Hidden Vietnam: Phong Nha Caves, Imperial Hue & Quy Nhon's Secret Beaches
subtitle: The world's largest caves, an imperial stopover, and beaches still off the radar
climate: Best period: February to May, temperatures 25-30°C, minimal rainfall. Avoid October-December at all costs (torrential rains, frequent flooding, possible typhoons). June-August is hot and humid (32-35°C) but manageable.

title: Wallis and Futuna: Lagoons, Crater Lakes and Tongan Marae
subtitle: Eight days between Wallis' turquoise lagoon, its 15th-century forts and Futuna's wild coasts, birthplace of Oceania's first martyr.
climate: Best time: May to October, temperatures 23-26°C, less rain. Avoid November to April: active cyclone season, heavy rainfall and oppressive heat (28-30°C). High humidity year-round.

title: Mayotte Journey: Giant Lagoon & Tropical Islets
subtitle: From Mamoudzou to the southern shores, exploring extinct volcanoes, mangroves, and sea turtle havens
climate: Best time: May to October (dry season), 24-28°C, ideal for lagoon activities. Avoid December-March: heavy rainfall, oppressive heat (30-33°C), and cyclone risk. November and April are transition months with variable weather.

title: Eastern Cape Road Trip, South Africa: Tsitsikamma to the Wild Coast
climate: Best time: March-May and September-November, temperatures 18-25°C along the coast, ideal for Tsitsikamma and Wild Coast. Avoid December-February (extreme heat inland, frequent thunderstorms) and June-August in Hogsback and Rhodes mountains (frost, challenging roads). Wild Coast remains wet year-round.

title: Free State, South Africa: Golden Sandstone, Craters & Highland Vistas
climate: Best time: September–November and March–May, temperatures 15–25°C, clear skies. Avoid June–August (frequent night frost at altitude, occasional snow at Golden Gate) and December–February (violent afternoon thunderstorms). Altitude amplifies day-night temperature swings.

title: Gauteng, South Africa: From Cradle of Humankind to Urban Safaris
climate: Best period: April-May and September-October, temperatures 18-25°C, clear skies. Avoid December-January (violent afternoon thunderstorms daily) and June-July (freezing nights, frequent frost despite sunny days). The altitude (1700m) moderates summer heat.

title: Limpopo, South Africa Safari: Waterberg Wilderness to Kruger National Park
climate: Best time: May-September (dry season), daytime temperatures 10-25°C, cool nights. Avoid December-February: extreme heat (35-40°C), violent storms, high humidity, increased malaria risk. October is scorching but excellent for wildlife viewing near waterholes.

title: Mpumalanga, South Africa: Blyde River Canyon and Kruger Safari Adventure
climate: Best time: May to September (dry season, 15-25°C, ideal for wildlife viewing in Kruger). Avoid December-February: heavy daily rainfall, oppressive heat (30-35°C), high humidity, and dense vegetation limiting animal sightings. The highlands (Blyde Canyon, Graskop) remain cooler but very humid in summer.

title: The Perfect South Africa Road Trip
subtitle: From Johannesburg to Cape Town via Kruger and the Garden Route — 14 days through the most breathtaking landscapes on the African continent.
climate: Best time: April-May and September-October, temperatures 18-28°C, ideal balance between Kruger (dry season) and Cape Town (avoiding winter rains). Avoid December-February in Kruger (intense heat 35-40°C, thunderstorms, dense vegetation for game viewing). Cape Town gets cold winter rains June-August, Johannesburg can freeze in winter.

title: Northern Cape, South Africa Odyssey: Diamond Mines to Starry Desert Skies
climate: Best time: March-May and September-November, temperatures 15-28°C, clear skies ideal for stargazing. Avoid December-February (extreme heat in the Kalahari, often >40°C). Nights cool to cold year-round in Sutherland, bring warm clothing.

title: North West South Africa Explorer: Ancient Mountains and Big Five Safaris
climate: Best time: May to September, with 20-25°C daytime temperatures, dry weather and optimal wildlife viewing. Avoid December-February (scorching heat 30-38°C, violent afternoon thunderstorms). Winter nights are cold (5-10°C): pack warm clothing for early morning game drives.

title: South Africa's Western Cape: Cape Town, Winelands & Garden Route
climate: Best time: September-April, temperatures 18-28°C on the coast. Avoid June-August (winter rains, cold weather). Expect strong south-easterly winds in Cape Town during summer, and extreme heat in the Karoo (Oudtshoorn) January-February (35°C+).

title: South Africa - 7 days from Cape Town to the Cape Winelands, the most French of New World wine countries
subtitle: Seven days between Cape Town, Stellenbosch, Franschhoek, Constantia and the Hemel-en-Aarde Valley - century-old Pinotage, Simonsberg Cabernet and whales at Hermanus.
climate: Best time: October-April (spring and summer), temperatures 20-28°C, perfect for wine country. Avoid June-August (rainy winter, overcast skies, 15°C). Strong south-easterly winds can blow in January-February in Cape Town.
